tips: Kauai is the most lush and least developed major Hawaiian island — expect a slower, more natural experience.
The drive from the North Shore (Hanalei) to the South Shore (Poipu) takes about 1 hour. Plan accordingly for South Shore activities.
Na Pali Coast is only accessible by boat, helicopter, or an 11-mile hike (Kalalau Trail). The boat tour is the best option for families.
Choose a catamaran over a zodiac for the Na Pali tour — smoother ride for kids and the grandparent who is afraid of turbulence/rough motion.
Poipu Beach is one of the best toddler beaches in Hawaii — protected cove, lifeguards, monk seals, and tide pools.
Kauai Backcountry tubing has a minimum age of 5 and minimum weight of 43 lbs — confirm the 5yo qualifies before booking.
Pack reef-safe sunscreen (required by Hawaii law) and reapply frequently.
1 Hotel Hanalei Bay reopened in 2023 after extensive renovation — it's Kauai's newest luxury option.
The Allerton Garden Jurassic Park connection will thrill the 5yo — show them the movie scene before the trip.
Bring a lightweight stroller for resort paths and a baby carrier for excursions and beaches.
Kilauea Lighthouse closes early — check hours and plan to arrive by mid-afternoon at the latest.
Consider a portable sound machine for nap times in the hotel room.
Hanalei Bay in summer has generally calm water, but always check conditions — winter brings big surf.

name: Channel Islands & Ojai, California
tagline: The 'North American Galapagos' — island foxes, sea caves, kelp forest snorkeling & zero jet lag
weather: 68–82°F, classic California summer — warm inland (Ojai), cooler on islands (60–70°F), fog possible mornings

outbound.route: LAX → Ojai via US-101 (1.5 hrs), then Ventura Harbor for island boats
outbound.class: Private luxury SUV or drive own car

return.class: Private luxury SUV or drive own car

hotel.name: Ojai Valley Inn + Ventura Beach Marriott (for island access days)

rooms.name: Ojai Valley Inn — Artist Cottage Suite (7 nights)
rooms.for: Adults + kids — 2-bedroom cottage with full kitchen, private patio, garden views, fireplace

rooms.name: Ojai Valley Inn — Spa Penthouse Suite (7 nights)
rooms.for: Grandparents — top floor, mountain views, soaking tub, close to Spa Ojai

highlights: 220-acre resort in the Topatopa Mountains — feels worlds away from LA
Apothecary spa — one of California's best
Camp Ojai kids' program (ages 5–12)
Artist Cottage & Studio arts programming for all ages
Two pools including a family splash zone
Golf, tennis, horseback riding on-site
Farm-to-table dining at Olivella
Only 30 min drive to Ventura Harbor for island boat departures

days.title: Arrival in Ojai — Pink Moment Welcome

schedule.activity: Depart LAX by car — scenic US-101 along the coast, then Hwy 33 into the Ojai Valley
Stop in Ventura — walk the pier, see sea lions sunbathing on the beach, quick taco at Beach House Tacos
Arrive Ojai Valley Inn, check into cottages — the mountain views are stunning
Lunch at Olivella restaurant (resort) — wood-fired pizza, Mediterranean salads, kids' pasta
Nap time — settle in, unpack, kids rest in their new room
Resort pool and splash zone — lazy afternoon, warm Ojai sun, mountain air
Walk to the Ojai Valley lookout for 'Pink Moment' — the mountains turn brilliant pink at sunset (Ojai's signature phenomenon)
Dinner at Azu Restaurant in Ojai town — tapas, paella, sangria for adults, kids' plates
Ojai Arcade walk — browse the charming downtown shops, bookstores, ice cream at Ojai Ice Cream

days.title: Santa Cruz Island — Island Foxes & Sea Caves

schedule.activity: Early breakfast at resort (grab-and-go)
Drive to Ventura Harbor (30 min), board Island Packers boat to Santa Cruz Island (1 hr crossing)
Arrive Scorpion Anchorage, Santa Cruz Island — Channel Islands National Park. Immediately spot island foxes! They're fearless and the size of house cats
Guided nature walk — island scrub jays (found nowhere else on Earth!), island foxes, native plants, tide pools
Sea cave kayaking with Island Adventures — the world's largest sea cave (Painted Cave) is here! Paddle through enormous sea caves with rock formations
Grandparents + toddlers: beach time at Scorpion Beach — rocky pools with hermit crabs, calm cove
Packed lunch on the beach — pre-prepared by Ojai Valley Inn (required — no food services on island)
Snorkeling in Scorpion Anchorage — kelp forest! Garibaldi fish (bright orange California state fish), sea stars, horn sharks in the kelp. The 5-year-old's kelp forest adventure!
Nap time on the beach — toddlers rest under shade while others explore tide pools nearby
Board return boat to Ventura (1 hr) — watch for dolphins on the crossing (common!)
Return to Ojai, rest and cleanup
Dinner at The Farmhouse at Ojai Valley Inn — seasonal farm-to-table, candlelit garden setting
Breakfast at Olivella
Ojai Certified Farmers' Market (Sunday market) — fresh produce, honey, baked goods, live music, kids love the samples
Ojai Lavender Festival activities (if coinciding, late June) or visit Ojai Olive Oil Company — taste olive oils, kids try dipping bread
Bart's Books — the world's largest outdoor bookstore! Browse under oak trees, kids find picture books, honor system coin boxes
Lunch at Farmer & the Cook — organic Mexican-inspired food, veggie burritos, smoothies, outdoor garden seating
Nap time at resort
Resort Artist Cottage & Studio — pottery painting, watercolor class, kids and grandparents create art together
Grandparents: Spa Ojai — signature treatment. Parents + kids: pool time and garden walk
Dinner at Nocciola in Ojai — Italian, handmade pasta, wood-fired everything
Drive to Lake Casitas overlook — watch sunset paint the mountains
Breakfast at resort
Drive to Ventura Harbor, board Island Packers whale-watching cruise (3.5 hrs) — blue whales feed in the Santa Barbara Channel June-July! Also humpbacks, dolphins, sea lions
Return to harbor, lunch at Brophy Bros — dockside, clam chowder in bread bowls, fish tacos, crab cakes
Nap time — back to Ojai, rest at resort
Camp Ojai afternoon for 5-year-old — outdoor adventure activities, nature exploration. Parents + toddlers at resort pool
Horseback riding at Ojai Valley Inn stables — gentle trail ride through the valley for adults/grandparents (kids watch the horses and feed them carrots)
Dinner at Osteria Monte Grappa in Ojai — rustic Italian, family-style portions, garden patio

days.title: Anacapa Island — Sea Lions & Kelp Forests

schedule.activity: Early breakfast
Drive to Ventura Harbor, board Island Packers to Anacapa Island (1 hr)
Arrive Anacapa — climb the iconic lighthouse staircase (154 steps, manageable with carriers for toddlers), panoramic views
Inspiration Point overlook — watch hundreds of sea lions on the rocks below, pelicans diving, possibly gray whales passing
Snorkeling in Landing Cove — giant kelp forest, bright orange Garibaldi fish, bat rays, leopard sharks (small, harmless, thrilling!)
Tide pool exploration — sea anemones, hermit crabs, sea stars, purple urchins. 5-year-old's marine biology lab
Packed lunch on the island — eat with the view of the Pacific
Return boat to Ventura — dolphins often ride the bow wake!
Nap time at resort
Lazy pool afternoon — decompress from island adventure
Dinner at Boccali's — beloved local spot, strawberry shortcake and pizza on the patio, mountain views
Breakfast at resort
Drive to Santa Barbara (40 min) — Santa Barbara Zoo, intimate hillside zoo with ocean views, giraffes, penguins, train ride, splash pad for toddlers
Santa Barbara Sea Center on Stearns Wharf — touch tanks with sharks, rays, sea urchins, live underwater camera feed, 5-year-old's shark fix!
Lunch at The Lark — one of SB's best restaurants, seasonal California cuisine, outdoor courtyard
Nap time — drive back to Ojai, rest at resort
Ojai Valley trail walk/bike — flat paved path through the valley, rent family bikes with kid trailers, ride past orchards and mountains
Pink Moment viewing from Meditation Mount — the best spot in Ojai for the sunset phenomenon
Dinner at The Ranch House — legendary Ojai garden restaurant, herb garden dining, fresh trout, seasonal menu
Late breakfast at resort — slow morning
Easy hike in Los Padres National Forest — Rose Valley Falls trail (1 mile, easy, shaded), waterfall at the end, 5-year-old leads the way
Visit Ojai Raptor Center — rescue birds of prey up close, hawks, owls, falcons. Guided educational tour
Lunch at Hip Vegan — surprisingly delicious plant-based comfort food, or Ojai Deer Lodge for burgers
Nap time at resort
Full family spa experience — Spa Ojai for grandparents (Kuyam treatment — mud, steam, meditation), parents get massage, kids get 'kids spa' experience (gentle foot soak, face mask)
Resort family photo session — golden hour in the gardens with the Topatopa Mountains behind
Farewell dinner at Olivella — multi-course celebration dinner, California wines, kids' tasting menu
S'mores and stargazing at the resort fire pit — clear Ojai skies, Milky Way visible
Final breakfast at Olivella
Last pool swim and garden walk — soak it in
Check out, load the car — wave goodbye to the mountains
Quick stop at Ojai Valley Brewing for growlers (adults) and Ojai Beverage Company for the drive
Arrive home in LA — no airport, no jet lag, no hassle. Kids barely noticed they left!

tips: This is the EASIEST destination for a family with small kids — no flights, no jet lag, no airport stress, no passports
Channel Islands boats can be rough — take Dramamine for the turbulence-fearing grandparent, sit in the middle of the boat
Pack layers for the islands — it's 10-15°F cooler than the mainland, plus wind on the boat
Island Packers sells out in summer — book the Santa Cruz and Anacapa trips weeks in advance
There are NO services on the islands — bring all food, water, sunscreen, and diapers
June-July is blue whale season in the Santa Barbara Channel — one of the best whale-watching spots on Earth
The Ojai Valley Inn is dog-friendly if you want to bring the family pet
This trip saves $10K-30K on flights alone compared to international destinations — reinvest in experiences

costBreakdown.hotel: $14,000 (7 nights × 2 suites at Ojai Valley Inn)

tagline: A French fairy-tale castle hotel, beluga whales in the St. Lawrence & Canadian farm country charm
weather: 65–78°F, pleasant Canadian summer, long daylight, occasional rain showers

Fairmont Gold lounge — complimentary breakfast, afternoon canapés, concierge

days.title: Arrival in Quebec City — The Castle Hotel

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on Air Canada Business Class to Montreal
Arrive Montreal, connect to Quebec City (1 hr flight)
Arrive Quebec City, private transfer to Fairmont Le Château Frontenac (20 min)
Check in — the 5-year-old will think you're staying in a real castle! Towers, turrets, grand hallways
Dinner at Champlain Restaurant in the Château — refined Quebec cuisine, duck confit, foie gras, kids' menu
Walk the Dufferin Terrace boardwalk outside the castle — stunning views of the St. Lawrence River, lit up at night
Breakfast in Fairmont Gold Lounge — pastries, eggs, fresh Quebec cheeses, kids' favorites
Walk the ramparts of Old Quebec — the only walled city in North America, cannons for kids to climb on, views everywhere
Ride the Funicular down to Quartier Petit-Champlain — the quaintest street in North America, murals, boutiques, buskers
Chocolaterie Érico — chocolate museum and shop, watch chocolate being made, taste samples, hot chocolate for everyone
Lunch at Le Cochon Dingue — beloved bistro, French onion soup, croque monsieur, crêpes for kids
Nap time at hotel

Breakfast at Fairmont Gold Lounge

Walk the base trail — feel the mist, see the rainbow in the spray, kids get soaked and love it
Drive to Île d'Orléans (10 min) — pastoral island in the St. Lawrence, farm stands, strawberry fields (late June is strawberry season!)
Pick-your-own strawberries at a local farm — kids fill baskets of sweet Quebec strawberries
Lunch at Panache Mobile food truck or Auberge Le Canard Huppé — duck and local farm produce
Nap time — drive around Île d'Orléans (toddlers sleep in car), adults enjoy vineyard and cheese shop stops
Cidrerie & Verger Bilodeau — apple orchard and cidery, cider tasting for adults, apple juice for kids, farm animals for petting
Return to Quebec City, rest at hotel
Dinner at Chez Muffy (Auberge Saint-Antoine) — farm-to-table Quebec cuisine in a 19th-century maritime warehouse
Breakfast at hotel
Aquarium du Québec — walruses, seals, polar bears, jellyfish tunnel, outdoor pools where kids can touch stingrays and starfish. 5-year-old's paradise!
Walk the outdoor exhibits — Arctic fox, caribou, black bear viewing areas
Lunch at Chez Victor — famous Quebec burger joint, poutine (the kids need to try real Quebec poutine!)
Nap time at hotel
Plains of Abraham — huge park on the cliffs, kids run and play, see where the famous battle happened, Discovery Pavilion has interactive kids' exhibits
Grandparents: spa at the Château's Moment Spa. Parents + kids: explore the hotel's secret passages and towers (the Château has guided family tours)
Dinner at Le Saint-Amour — one of Quebec City's finest, French cuisine, intimate setting, early family reservation
Breakfast, check out of Château Frontenac
Scenic drive along Route 138 to Charlevoix (1.5 hrs) — St. Lawrence River views the whole way, mountains rising
Stop in Baie-Saint-Paul — charming art village, galleries, Laiterie Charlevoix (cheese factory and ice cream)
Lunch at Le Mouton Noir in Baie-Saint-Paul — local farm ingredients, lamb, garden vegetables
Drive to La Malbaie, check into Fairmont Le Manoir Richelieu, nap time
Explore the hotel grounds — indoor pool, casino (adults), massive lawns overlooking the river, kids run free
Walk to the Charlevoix lookout — dramatic cliff views of the St. Lawrence, mountains, and whale-watching territory below
Dinner at Le Charlevoix restaurant (hotel) — regional tasting menu, Charlevoix lamb, local cheeses, kids' options
Early breakfast — whale-watching departure is early!
Drive to Tadoussac (1.5 hrs including free ferry crossing at Baie-Sainte-Catherine)
Whale-watching Zodiac boat tour with Croisières AML — beluga whales, minke whales, fin whales, humpbacks! Tadoussac is one of the world's best spots. Life jackets for all
schedule.who: All (5yo+ on Zodiac, or larger boat for toddlers)

schedule.activity: Marine Mammal Interpretation Centre — learn about St. Lawrence whales, whale skeleton, kid-friendly displays
Lunch at Café Bohème in Tadoussac — overlooking the bay, seafood chowder, sandwiches
Nap time — drive back toward La Malbaie, toddlers sleep in car
Stop at Les Jardins de Cap-à-l'Aigle — beautiful riverside gardens, gentle walk, lily ponds
Resort pool time — indoor/outdoor pool, hot tub, kids' area
Dinner at La Pinsonnière — one of Charlevoix's finest restaurants, overlooking the river, tasting menu
Breakfast at hotel
Le Train de Charlevoix scenic railway — ride the vintage train along the St. Lawrence shoreline from La Malbaie to Baie-Saint-Paul (1.5 hrs). Kids love trains!
Arrive Baie-Saint-Paul — visit La Ferme Basque farm, see ducks and geese, taste duck foie gras (adults), pet animals (kids)
Lunch at Maison de la Ferme — farm restaurant, organic produce, wood-fired oven dishes
Train back to La Malbaie, nap time at hotel
Sentier des Caps hiking trail (easy section) — cliff-top boardwalk through the forest above the St. Lawrence, look for bald eagles
Fromagerie Saint-Fidèle — artisan cheese tasting, see the cheese-making room, buy supplies for a sunset picnic
Farewell picnic on the Manoir Richelieu lawn — Quebec cheeses, bread, charcuterie, wine, watching the sunset over the St. Lawrence
Pack and prepare for departure
Early breakfast at hotel
Drive from La Malbaie to Quebec City airport (1.5 hrs)
Depart YQB → Montreal YUL, connect to LAX
Connect at Montreal, Air Canada lounge
Depart YUL → LAX on Air Canada Business Class
Arrive LAX — au revoir, Quebec!

tips: Quebec City is incredibly safe — one of the lowest crime rates in North America
Everyone speaks French but most people also speak English — great exposure for kids to hear French
The Château Frontenac literally looks like a Disney castle — 5-year-old will be in awe from the moment you arrive
Late June is strawberry season in Quebec — pick-your-own is a must
Whale watching in Tadoussac is best June-October, with belugas present year-round
Poutine is the ultimate kid food — French fries, cheese curds, and gravy
Book the Zodiac whale tour for the closest encounters, but the larger boats are better for toddlers and grandparents
No passport hassle — US visitors need passport but Canada entry is smooth

weather: 82–90°F, hot and humid, brief afternoon showers, sea turtle nesting season

Rosewood Explorers kids club (ages 4–12)

tips: Whale shark season peaks June–September — book through a certified operator (max 2 swimmers per guide, life jackets required)
Turtle nesting season is June–October — Rosewood has a dedicated marine biology team that leads guided patrols
Cenotes are freshwater and can be cool (75°F) — refreshing in the heat but bring a rash guard for little ones
The 4.5-hour nonstop flight is the easiest of all A-tier destinations — great for the turbulence-averse grandparent
June–July is technically rainy season but showers are brief (30 min afternoon), mornings are sunny
Rosewood's boat shuttle between villas feels like a private theme park ride — kids adore it
Stay on resort property at night — Playa del Carmen is generally safe but avoid wandering unfamiliar areas after dark

Guided reef snorkeling at the barrier reef (just offshore) with Big Blue Unlimited — 5-year-old in life vest, grandparents can glass-bottom kayak
Lunch at Bugaloo's Conch Crawl — waterside shack, famous conch fritters and conch salad
Nap time back at resort — toddlers sleep, grandparents pool lounge
Resort pool time — kids' splash area, swim lessons for 5-year-old available
Grandparents spa — deep tissue massage at Shore Club spa

Evening walk along Grace Bay, spot hermit crabs on the beach
Breakfast at resort
Private boat to Little Water Cay (Iguana Island) — boardwalk trail to see endangered Turks & Caicos rock iguanas up close. Stroller-friendly boardwalk
Sandbar stop at Half Moon Bay — knee-deep turquoise water, natural wading pool perfect for toddlers and the 1-year-old
Lunch at Da Conch Shack — legendary beachside spot, fresh cracked conch, jerk chicken
Nap time at resort
Beach play at Grace Bay — sand castles, wade in the warm shallows
Light early dinner at Kasa Japanese restaurant (at resort) — edamame, tempura, sushi rolls
Glow Worm Cruise with Caicos Dream Tours — witness bioluminescent worms light up the ocean 3 days after full moon (June/July timing is perfect!)
Return to resort, kids to bed, adults stargazing from terrace
Breakfast at Colonnade
Caicos Conch Farm tour — the world's only conch farm, see conch at every life stage, hold baby conchs (5-year-old will be fascinated)
Sapodilla Bay Beach — the calmest, shallowest beach on the island, ankle-deep water extends 100 yards out, perfect for toddler wading
Lunch at Las Brisas Restaurant — oceanfront, Caribbean fare, fish tacos
Nap time at resort
Paddleboard or kayak in the mangrove channels with Big Blue Unlimited — spot baby sea turtles, nurse sharks, rays
Grandparents with toddlers at resort pool and splash pad
Sunset catamaran cruise along Grace Bay — champagne, juice boxes, snacks on board
Dinner at Parallel23 (The Palms resort) — elevated Caribbean cuisine, lobster tails, ocean breeze dining
Early breakfast at resort, pack snacks for day trip
Drive to Leeward marina, take ferry to North Caicos (30 min smooth crossing)
Wade Creek wetlands — flamingo spotting in the wild! Pink flamingos in shallow lagoons
Mudjin Harbour, Middle Caicos — dramatic limestone cliffs, caves, and a stunning beach. Easy walk down to the sand
Lunch at Daniel's Café in Conch Bar — home-cooked island food, cracked lobster
Conch Bar Caves — the largest limestone cave system in the Caribbean, easy walk, bats (exciting for 5-year-old!)
Ferry back to Provo, nap time in transit and at resort
Quiet beach time at Grace Bay — late afternoon is magical light
Dinner at Infiniti Restaurant & Raw Bar — rooftop dining, fresh sashimi, lobster mac & cheese for kids
Breakfast at resort
Semi-submarine reef tour with Caicos Tours — stay dry while viewing coral reef through glass hull, perfect for grandparents and toddlers
Smith's Reef snorkeling (walk-in from shore) — sea turtles, stingrays, reef fish. 5-year-old's best snorkel spot
Grandparents + toddlers: resort Kids' Club and pool
Lunch at Sharkbite Bar & Grill — aptly named for our shark lover! Grouper sandwiches
Nap time at resort
Full family spa afternoon — grandparents get full treatments, parents get chair massages, kids get 'Little Spa' mini pedicures
Beach time — final Grace Bay golden hour
Dinner at Grace's Cottage — romantic garden setting, pre-fixe Caribbean menu, kids' early seating
Night beach walk — search for bioluminescence in the sand when disturbed
Leisurely breakfast — in-suite with room service
Chalk Sound National Park — stunning turquoise lagoon, paddleboard or kayak through impossibly blue water dotted with tiny islands
Taylor Bay Beach — another ultra-shallow, calm beach ideal for the 1-year-old to splash
Lunch at Baci Ristorante — Italian-Caribbean fusion, wood-fired pizza kids love
Nap time at resort
Resort beach afternoon — build a mega sandcastle, boogie boarding for 5-year-old in gentle waves
Family photo session on Grace Bay beach at golden hour — hotel photographer
Farewell celebration dinner at Stelle (Gansevoort) — beachfront, lobster and steak, tasting menu for adults
Final sunset from resort terrace, pack bags
Early breakfast at Colonnade
Final beach walk — let kids splash one more time
Check out, private transfer to PLS airport
Depart PLS → Miami, connect to LAX
Connect in Miami — kids lunch in Admirals Club lounge
Toddlers nap on MIA→LAX flight
Arrive LAX — vacation complete!

tips: Grace Bay's water is bathtub-calm most days — perfect for nervous waders and toddlers
Book the glow worm cruise for 3-4 days after a full moon — that's when they appear
Baby supplies available at IGA and Graceway supermarkets on Providenciales
The island has excellent medical facilities — Grace Bay Medical Centre is 5 minutes away
Rent a car for day trips — driving is on the left (British territory) but roads are easy
June-July is slightly off-peak, meaning fewer crowds but still perfect weather
Baby shark sightings at Grace Bay are most common in early morning — head out at sunrise

tagline: Sloths in the rainforest, volcano views from your hot spring, and howler monkeys as your alarm clock
weather: 75–88°F, green season (short afternoon showers, lush scenery, fewer crowds)

rooms.name: Four Seasons: Tropical Garden View Room (King)
Nayara Gardens: Arenal Casita Deluxe with plunge pool
Nayara Gardens: Arenal Casita with plunge pool

Three pools at Four Seasons including adults-only
Nayara private plunge pool in every casita
Volcano views from Nayara casitas
Farm-to-table dining at both properties
On-site spa with hot springs at Nayara

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on Alaska Airlines
Arrive Liberia airport
Private van transfer to Four Seasons Papagayo (35 min)
Early check-in, breakfast at Caracol restaurant
Pool time — main pool with kids splash area
Lunch at Pesce (oceanview seafood)
Nap time
Beach walk on Virador Beach — calm waters, tide pools
Dinner at Anejo (Latin-inspired cuisine, outdoor terrace)
Breakfast at Caracol
Guided nature walk on resort trails — howler monkeys, iguanas, tropical birds, coatis
Kayaking in Culebra Bay (calm waters)
Toddlers at kids club with nanny
Lunch at Bahia pool bar & grill
Nap time

Chocolate & rum tasting (adults) while kids have smoothie workshop
Dinner at Caracol (Costa Rican specials night)
Breakfast at Caracol
Half-day catamaran cruise along Papagayo coast — snorkeling, dolphin spotting, onboard lunch

Visit Sloth Sanctuary or Bogarin Trail — see two-toed and three-toed sloths up close in natural habitat
Arenal Hanging Bridges — walk through cloud forest canopy on suspension bridges (stroller-friendly main path)
Lunch at El Novillo del Arenal (local steakhouse, Costa Rican casado)

Hot springs afternoon at Nayara — thermal pools of different temperatures

Lunch at Don Rufino (La Fortuna town, upscale Costa Rican)
Nap time at resort
Free time — private plunge pool, spa, or birding from casita deck
Dinner at Amor Loco (farewell dinner, chef's tasting)

days.title: Zip Lines & Transfer to San José

Sky Adventures Arenal — zip line canopy tour for adults and 5yr old (min age 4). Toddlers and grandparents do the Sky Tram (aerial gondola) for volcano views

tips: June–July is green season — expect short afternoon rain showers, but mornings are usually clear and prices are 30–40% lower
Request a Pack-n-Play and baby supplies from both hotels in advance

Four Seasons Papagayo has complimentary kids club ages 4–12, so the 5-year-old can participate

costBreakdown.flights: $24,400 (6 purchased seats first/business class ~$4,000/pp avg across two legs + ~$400 lap infant fee)
costBreakdown.activities: $4,000 (catamaran, hanging bridges, volcano park, zip lines, sloth sanctuary, waterfall)
costBreakdown.food: $5,500 (8 days, mix of resort and local)

name: Hokkaido, Japan
tagline: Lavender fields, bear parks, blue ponds, and the freshest sushi on Earth — Japan's wild north island
weather: 60–77°F, warm but not hot, low humidity (unlike mainland Japan), long daylight
flightTime: ~11.5 hrs LAX–HND + 1.5 hr domestic to CTS (Sapporo)

outbound.class: ANA Business Class (The Room) + Domestic Premium

return.class: JAL Business Class (Sky Suite) + Domestic First

hotel.name: The Windsor Hotel Toya Resort & Spa (Lake Toya, 3 nights) + Furano Prince Hotel (3 nights)

rooms.name: Windsor: Lake View Family Suite (tatami area + western beds)
Furano Prince: Family Room (western beds + extra futons)
Furano Prince: Twin Room with Lavender View

highlights: Windsor Hotel: perched above Lake Toya with volcano views, outdoor onsen, Michel Bras restaurant
Furano Prince: in the heart of lavender country, ski-resort style but summer flowers
Both hotels extremely child-friendly (Japan standard)
Onsen (hot spring baths) at both locations
Hokkaido is Japan's food capital — dairy, seafood, melon, corn

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on ANA
Arrive Tokyo Haneda, connect to domestic flight to New Chitose (Sapporo)
Arrive Sapporo New Chitose Airport, rent a van
Quick breakfast at airport — try Hokkaido milk soft serve (famous even at the airport)
Drive to Lake Toya (2 hrs — scenic route through countryside)
Kids nap in car
Arrive Windsor Hotel Toya, check in, settle into rooms
Lunch at Windsor Grill (lake views, Hokkaido beef)
Rest — jet lag nap for everyone
Walk the hotel grounds, first views of Lake Toya and Mount Usu volcano
Dinner at hotel Japanese restaurant (kaiseki — multicourse Japanese)
Evening onsen bath (family private onsen available, great for kids)
Breakfast at hotel buffet (incredible Hokkaido dairy, fresh pastries)
Drive to Noboribetsu (45 min)
Noboribetsu Bear Park (Kuma Bokujo) — take the ropeway up the mountain, see Hokkaido brown bears up close in naturalistic enclosures, feed them snacks from above. Kids absolutely love this.
Walk through Jigokudani (Hell Valley) — volcanic hot springs, steaming vents, colorful mineral deposits (paved path, stroller-ok for first section)
Lunch at a Noboribetsu onsen town restaurant — try Hokkaido ramen (miso butter corn ramen is the local specialty)
Nap time (drive back or rest at a day-onsen)
Lake Toya boat cruise — sightseeing boat around the volcanic islands in the caldera lake (1 hr, smooth)
Free time at hotel — playground, pool, or onsen
Dinner at Michel Bras Toya (French-Japanese fusion, Michelin-starred, in the Windsor Hotel — possibly most memorable meal of the trip)
Kids dinner at hotel kids menu / room service with babysitter
Breakfast at hotel
Mount Usu Ropeway — ride to the summit for views of the volcanic crater, Lake Toya, and the Pacific Ocean. Easy walking path at top.
Visit the Volcano Science Museum in Toyako town (interactive, kid-friendly exhibits about the 2000 eruption)
Walk the Toyako lakefront promenade — outdoor sculptures, playgrounds, lake views
Lunch at a lakeside café in Toyako town (try Hokkaido corn and potato dishes)
Nap time at hotel
Family onsen time at Windsor (private family bath)
Pack for tomorrow's transfer to Furano
Farewell dinner at Windsor — sushi counter (omakase with Hokkaido uni, crab, and salmon)
Lake Toya fireworks (nightly from late April through October — visible from hotel room)

days.title: Transfer to Furano — Blue Pond & Lavender

schedule.activity: Breakfast and check out of Windsor
Drive to Furano via the scenic inland route (~3.5 hrs with stops)
Stop at Biei — visit the famous Blue Pond (Aoiike). Electric blue water from mineral deposits, surrounded by birch forest. Easy flat boardwalk.
Drive the Patchwork Hills of Biei — rolling hills of different colored crops (lavender, wheat, potatoes) looking like a quilt
Lunch at Biei farm café (farm-fresh lunch, potato croquettes, corn soup)
Continue to Furano, check into Furano Prince Hotel
Nap time
Visit Farm Tomita — Japan's most famous lavender farm. Early-bloom lavender in late June/early July. Walk the colorful flower fields, lavender soft serve ice cream.
Furano Wine Factory — wine tasting for adults, grape juice for kids, hilltop views
Dinner at Kumagera (Furano, famous for Hokkaido curry and mountain hut atmosphere)
Breakfast at hotel
Drive to Asahiyama Zoo in Asahikawa (1 hr) — Japan's most famous zoo, known for innovative exhibits: penguin walk-through tunnel, polar bear underwater viewing, snow leopard house
Lunch at zoo or Asahikawa city (try Asahikawa ramen — soy sauce based, different from Sapporo style)
Nap time on drive back
Stop at Lavender East (Farm Tomita's second farm, open June 20–July 20) — vast fields of purple
Furano Cheese Factory — watch cheese being made, try fresh Hokkaido cheese and ice cream
Back at hotel, free time — onsen soak
Dinner at Furano Yamaoka (izakaya, local Hokkaido dishes — grilled lamb jingisukan)
Breakfast at hotel
Visit Furano Melon Farm — taste the famous Yubari melon (in season, world's most prized melon). Kids can pick fruit.
Drive to Shikisai-no-Oka (Hill of Seasonal Colors) — rainbow rows of flowers stretching across rolling hills. Ride the tractor-pulled cart through the fields.
Lunch at Biei countryside restaurant
Nap time
Drive back toward Sapporo (2 hrs), stop at Otaru (charming port town) if time allows — canal walk, music box museum, sushi street
Check into Sapporo hotel near train station (JR Tower Hotel Nikko Sapporo or similar)
Farewell dinner at Sapporo Nijo Market area — fresh sushi, Hokkaido crab, and uni at Tanuki Koji restaurant row
Stroll Sapporo Odori Park (July is beer garden festival season)
Early wake-up, taxi to New Chitose Airport (40 min)
Breakfast at airport (New Chitose has incredible food: Ramen Alley, Royce chocolate shop, Kinotoya cheese tarts)
Depart CTS → HND on JAL domestic
Arrive Haneda, connect to international flight
Depart HND on JAL to LAX
Arrive LAX same morning (cross date line)

tips: Lavender peaks mid-July, but early bloom starts late June — Farm Tomita will have color by your visit, just not full peak
Hokkaido is Japan's least crowded and most spacious island — perfect for families who want Japan without Tokyo intensity
Private family onsen (kashikiri buro) solves the problem of bathing with toddlers — available at most hotels, book in advance
Japanese convenience stores (7-Eleven, Lawson) are lifesavers for baby food, snacks, and drinks — far superior to US convenience stores
The 11.5-hour flight to Tokyo is long but ANA's The Room business class has doors and lie-flat beds — much easier with the baby
Rent a van, not a sedan — you need space for 7 people, car seats, and strollers. Japanese rental companies offer child seats.
New Chitose Airport has a Royce chocolate factory tour and a Doraemon museum inside the terminal — perfect for early arrivals
Hokkaido summer weather (60–77°F) is dramatically cooler and less humid than mainland Japan — ideal for grandparents and toddlers

costBreakdown.flights: $42,650 (6 purchased seats business class round-trip LAX-HND at ~$6,500/pp + domestic CTS flights ~$500/pp x 6 + ~$650 lap infant fee)
costBreakdown.hotel: $8,310 (Windsor: ($550 + $350) x 3 = $2,700 + Furano Prince: ($275 + $210) x 3 = $1,455 + Sapporo hotel: $500 + rental van: $3,655)
costBreakdown.activities: $2,500 (bear park, zoo, ropeway rides, farm entries, boat cruise, volcano museum)
costBreakdown.food: $5,000 (7 days, 7 people — Hokkaido food is excellent value compared to Tokyo)
costBreakdown.transfers: $3,500 (rental van 7 days + gas + tolls + airport parking)

name: Bermuda
tagline: Pink sand, crystal caves & Bermuda Triangle shark legends — paradise in just 5 hours
weather: 78–85°F, warm and sunny with ocean breezes, occasional brief tropical showers

hotel.name: Rosewood Bermuda

rooms.name: Ocean Estate Suite (3-bedroom)
rooms.for: Adults + kids — interconnecting rooms with living area and kitchenette

rooms.name: Manor Premium Suite

Sense Spa for grandparents
Six pools including toddler splash area
Kids' Explorers Club (ages 3–12)
Complimentary electric Twizy cars for island exploration
In-room baby amenities and cribs

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on JetBlue Mint to JFK

Baby supplies (diapers, formula) available at Lindo's Market in Hamilton

The one-stop via JFK is the smoothest routing — direct flights only from East Coast cities

name: Aruba
tagline: One Happy Island — turquoise water, wild flamingos & trade-wind breezes outside the hurricane belt
weather: 85–90°F, sunny and dry — outside the hurricane belt, constant trade winds keep it comfortable

hotel.name: The Ritz-Carlton, Aruba

rooms.name: Oceanfront Two-Bedroom Suite
rooms.for: Adults + kids — separate living area, kitchenette, two bathrooms, cribs and rollaway beds pre-arranged

rooms.name: Deluxe Ocean-View Room (Club Level)
rooms.for: Grandparents — Club Lounge access with complimentary breakfast and evening cocktails, quieter floor

highlights: Direct access to Palm Beach — calm, shallow water perfect for toddlers
Ritz Kids program (ages 4–12) with nature activities and crafts
Two-level resort pool with lazy river, splash pad, and hot tub
On-site casino for adults-only evening entertainment
Spa & fitness center — great for grandparent relaxation days
In-room cribs, bottle warmers, and baby amenities on request
Multiple dining options including BLT Steak and poolside café

days.title: Arrival — Welcome to One Happy Island

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on American Airlines First Class to Miami
Arrive Miami, connect to Aruba flight — kids stretch legs in terminal play area
Arrive Queen Beatrix International Airport, private transfer to Ritz-Carlton (15 min)
Check in, settle kids into suites — cribs and toddler beds pre-arranged
Light dinner at the resort's poolside café — kids' menu, casual and quick after a long travel day
Bedtime — everyone rest up, trade winds through the balcony doors
Breakfast at the resort — fresh tropical fruit, pancakes for the kids, Aruban coffee
Palm Beach morning — turquoise calm water, white sand, toddlers splash in the shallows while grandparents set up under a palapa
Resort pool time — lazy river, splash pad for the kids, adults rotate for cocktails at the swim-up bar
Lunch at BLT Steak (resort) — burgers, chicken tenders for kids, fresh catch for adults
Nap time — toddlers and baby nap in the suite, grandparents rest or visit the spa
Beach walk toward the high-rise strip — 5-year-old hunts for shells and hermit crabs along the shore
Sunset happy hour at the resort's beach bar — mocktails for kids, Balashi beers for adults
Dinner at Barefoot Restaurant — toes-in-the-sand dining, fresh seafood, romantic and family-friendly at the same time
Bedtime routine at the resort — bath and books, kids asleep by 9, adults try the casino
Breakfast at the resort
Drive to Renaissance Marina in Oranjestad (10 min), take private water taxi to Renaissance Island
Flamingo Beach — wild pink flamingos walk right up to you! Hand-feed them and take photos. The 5-year-old will be mesmerized. Baby-friendly calm lagoon area
Explore Iguana Beach (the family-friendly side) — iguanas sunbathe on the rocks, shallow warm water for toddlers
Lunch on Renaissance Island — casual beach grill with island favorites
Water taxi back to Oranjestad, return to resort for nap time
Pool time at the resort — kids splash, grandparents lounge with books
Short drive to California Lighthouse for sunset — panoramic views of the entire island, photo-worthy spot
Dinner at Papiamento — historic Aruban cunucu house with courtyard dining, local and Dutch-Caribbean cuisine
Breakfast at the resort, pack snacks and sunscreen for a south-end adventure
Drive to Baby Beach (30 min south) — knee-deep crystal-clear water stretches out for hundreds of feet, like a giant natural kiddie pool. All 3 kids can splash safely
Snorkel the reef at the far end of Baby Beach — parents take turns with the 5-year-old while grandparents watch toddlers in the shallows
Lunch at a local fish shack near Baby Beach — fresh grilled mahi-mahi, Dutch croquettes, cold drinks
Drive back toward resort, nap time in the car and continue at the hotel
Philip's Animal Garden — a rescue zoo with monkeys, exotic birds, kangaroos, ostriches, coatimundis, and more. The 5-year-old will absolutely love this. Interactive and hands-on
Back to resort for pool cool-down and showers
Dinner at The Old Cunucu House — authentic Aruban food in a 150-year-old cottage, goat stew, fresh fish, and plantains
Bedtime — kids down, adults stroll the resort grounds or visit the casino
Early breakfast — fuel up for an adventure morning
Drive to Arikok National Park (20 min) — covers nearly 20% of the island, dramatic desert landscapes, giant boulders, and cactus forests
Guided nature walk — spot wild donkeys, iguanas, whiptail lizards, and Aruban burrowing owls (shoco). Visit Fontein Cave to see ancient Arawak cave paintings
Natural Pool (Conchi) viewpoint — the dramatic rock-enclosed ocean pool. With little kids, enjoy the views and wade at the calmer approaches rather than the deep pool itself
Drive to Boca Prins — wild windward coast, dramatic dunes, great for photos
Lunch at resort, then nap time — everyone recharges after a big morning
Butterfly Farm — hundreds of tropical butterflies land on you in an enclosed garden. Magical for all ages, especially the toddler and 5-year-old
Eagle Beach — often ranked #1 beach in the Caribbean. Iconic fofoti (divi-divi) trees, wide white sand, calm turquoise water. Perfect late-afternoon swim
Dinner at Screaming Eagle — upscale, creative fusion cuisine, bed-style cabana seating (reserve early, it's a unique experience)
Quiet evening at the resort — stargazing from the balcony, kids to bed
Breakfast at the resort
Ferry to De Palm Island (all-inclusive private island) — water slides, snorkeling lagoon, banana boats, and the famous Sea Trek underwater helmet walk
Kids' water park — slides and splash zones perfect for all ages, shallow wading pools for toddlers and baby
Snorkeling in the lagoon — colorful reef fish, sea stars. 5-year-old tries a mask and snorkel for the first time in calm, protected water
All-inclusive lunch on the island — BBQ, salads, tropical drinks included
Nap time — find a shady palapa, baby and toddler nap in stroller while grandparents relax on beach loungers
Sea Trek underwater helmet walk (adults) — walk on the ocean floor and see fish and coral without getting your hair wet. Grandparent-friendly, no swimming required

Dinner at Atardi (Ritz-Carlton) — beachfront sunset dining, great steaks and seafood, kids' menu
Breakfast at the resort

Atlantis Submarines tour — descend 130 feet in a real submarine! See shipwrecks, tropical fish, and coral reefs through large portholes. The 5-year-old will think this is the coolest thing ever. Air-conditioned and smooth — great for the grandparent afraid of turbulence (no waves felt underwater!)

schedule.activity: Early breakfast at the resort, final check of rooms

tips: Aruba is outside the hurricane belt — virtually no risk of tropical storms in June/July, making it one of the safest Caribbean picks for this time of year
The constant trade winds (15-20 mph) make 85-90°F feel very comfortable and keep mosquitoes at bay
Baby Beach is the #1 must-do for families with toddlers — the water is knee-deep for hundreds of feet and bathtub-warm
Flamingo Beach on Renaissance Island requires a day pass or Renaissance hotel booking — book the VIP cabana in advance for the family side
The submarine tour is fully air-conditioned and you don't feel any ocean waves once submerged — perfect for the grandparent who's afraid of turbulence
Aruba uses the Aruban florin but US dollars are accepted literally everywhere — no need to exchange currency
Tap water is safe to drink (desalinated) — one of the few Caribbean islands where this is the case
Rent a car for the Baby Beach and Arikok days — everything else is a short taxi or hotel shuttle ride
Philip's Animal Garden is a local gem that most tourists miss — the 5-year-old will talk about it for months

costBreakdown.activities: $3,500 (De Palm Island, submarine, catamaran, Flamingo Beach passes, Butterfly Farm, Philip's Animal Garden)
costBreakdown.transfers: $800 (airport transfers, car rental 2 days, water taxis)

tips: You lose a day flying to Fiji (cross the International Date Line) and gain it back on return — plan for only 6 full island days

tips: The Eisriesenwelt ice cave is genuinely 32°F inside — bring winter jackets, hats, and gloves even in July! Not recommended for the 1-year-old.
