Final ryokan breakfast in Takayama
Private transfer to Kamikochi via Hirayu Onsen (1.5 hrs) — stunning mountain pass drive
Arrive Kamikochi — walk across iconic Kappa Bridge over the crystal-clear Azusa River, Japanese Alps towering above
Easy nature walk along Azusa River boardwalk — wildflowers, butterflies, possible monkey sightings, fully flat and stroller-friendly
Check into Kamikochi Imperial Hotel, lunch at the hotel dining room — Western and Japanese options with mountain views
Nap time — toddlers sleep, adults rest on the hotel terrace with coffee and mountain air
Taisho Pond walk (30 min easy trail) — see dead trees standing in water reflecting the mountains, otherworldly beauty
Hot spring bath at hotel — relax alpine-weary muscles
Dinner at Kamikochi Imperial — French-Japanese fusion with local ingredients, kids' Western set
Breakfast at hotel — Western and Japanese buffet
Morning walk to Myojin Pond — sacred shrine on a mirror-still pond, Japanese macaques (snow monkeys) often seen along the trail
Myojin Shrine — toss a coin, make a wish, see the iwana (char) fish swimming in the crystal spring
River pebble play — kids throw stones in the Azusa River, wade in shallow edges (cold but refreshing!)
Lunch at Tokusawa Lodge (riverside picnic spot) — onigiri rice balls and thermos of miso soup packed by hotel
Nap time — return to hotel, rest
Guided nature walk with park ranger — learn about alpine flora, spot ptarmigan and Japanese serow (goat-antelope)
Watercolor painting session at hotel — mountain scenery painting for 5-year-old and grandparents
Dinner at hotel — kaiseki-style with river fish and mountain vegetables
Early breakfast, one final mountain walk — morning mist on Kappa Bridge is magical
Depart Kamikochi, private transfer back toward Takayama via Okuhida Onsen villages
Stop at Shinhotaka Ropeway — Japan's only double-decker gondola, ride to 7,000 ft for panoramic views of Northern Alps
Lunch at a mountain-top café — soba noodles with alpine views
Nap time — drive back to Takayama, check into ryokan, rest
Takayama Showa Museum — retro 1950s–60s Japan, vintage toys, old candy shop. 5-year-old can try old-fashioned Japanese games
Sake brewery tour on Sanmachi Suji — adults taste Hida sake, kids get amazake (sweet non-alcoholic rice drink)
Farewell kaiseki dinner at ryokan — the finest seasonal multi-course meal
Evening stroll through lantern-lit old town streets
Early breakfast, check out of ryokan
Limited express train Takayama → Nagoya, then Shinkansen to Tokyo (arrive ~12:30 PM)
Arrive Tokyo, check bags at hotel (Aman Tokyo or Palace Hotel), quick lunch at Tsukiji Outer Market — fresh sushi, tamagoyaki, melon pan
Nap time at hotel — toddlers sleep, grandparents rest
Ueno Zoo — Japan's oldest zoo, pandas, gorillas, tigers. 5-year-old's dream. Stroller-friendly
Quick walk through Ameyoko Market — bustling street market under the train tracks, candy stalls, toy shops
Dinner at Gonpachi Nishi-Azabu (the 'Kill Bill' restaurant) — yakitori, tempura, soba in a dramatic setting
Walk through Shinjuku's neon-lit streets (brief — kids in strollers), then back to hotel
Hotel breakfast
teamLab Borderless (Azabudai Hills) — immersive digital art that kids go wild for, touch-responsive projections, crawl-through spaces
Hakuhinkan Toy Park in Ginza — 4 floors of Japanese toys, the ultimate souvenir stop for the 5-year-old
Lunch at a family-friendly ramen shop near Ginza — tonkotsu ramen, gyoza
Transfer to Narita Airport via Narita Express (1 hr)
Clear security, ANA lounge — showers, kids' play area, hot food
Depart NRT on ANA Business Class to LAX — overnight flight, kids sleep
Arrive LAX same day (date line magic!)

tips: Japan is incredibly baby-friendly — changing tables and nursing rooms in nearly every public facility
Rent a stroller at airports or buy a cheap lightweight one at Nishimatsuya (baby store chain)
Takayama's morning markets have free samples everywhere — great for picky kid eaters
Kamikochi is car-free — only accessible by bus or approved private transfer
The one-grandparent-afraid-of-turbulence note: the LAX→Tokyo route is very smooth (over ocean), and the Shinkansen has zero turbulence of course
Pack layers — Kamikochi can be 55°F in the mornings even in summer
Japanese convenience stores (7-Eleven, Lawson) stock baby food, diapers, and formula — incredibly convenient

name: Mauritius
tagline: Giant tortoises, undersea walks, swimming with dolphins & the magical Seven Colored Earth
weather: 72–80°F, Mauritian winter (dry season!) — warm days, cooler evenings, least humid time of year

outbound.route: LAX → DXB (Dubai) → MRU (Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Airport, Mauritius)

hotel.name: One&Only Le Saint Géran

rooms.name: Grand Beach Suite (2-bedroom)
rooms.for: Adults + kids — beachfront, two bedrooms, spacious living room, private terrace, direct beach access

rooms.name: Beach Junior Suite
rooms.for: Grandparents — ground floor, beach views, easy access to pool and dining

highlights: Set on its own peninsula — private beach wrapping around the resort
KidsOnly Club (ages 3–11) with daily programs
PADI dive center and water sports center
Spa by Guerlain
Three restaurants including Tapasake overwater dining
Baby concierge service — cribs, monitors, warmers, strollers
Calm lagoon on one side, open ocean on the other

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on Emirates Business Class to Dubai — A380, onboard lounge, flat beds
Arrive Dubai, 3-hour layover in Emirates Business Lounge — showers, kids' play area, fresh food
Board Dubai → Mauritius flight (6.5 hrs) — overnight, kids sleep
Arrive Mauritius, private transfer to One&Only Le Saint Géran (45 min)
Check in, everyone to bed immediately — suites pre-set with cribs and night lights
Late breakfast at PRIME restaurant — Mauritian tropical breakfast, fresh tropical fruits, eggs, pastries
Explore the resort — walk the peninsula, discover the lagoon beach (perfect for toddlers), meet the resort's giant tortoises
Lunch at Tapasake — overwater restaurant, Japanese-inspired tapas, sushi, tempura
Nap time — recover from travel, lagoon sounds lull everyone to sleep
Lagoon swim — the resort's lagoon is bathwater-warm, shallow, and crystal clear. Toddlers splash, 5-year-old spots fish
Sunset cocktails at the bar — rum punch for adults, tropical smoothies for kids, watch the Indian Ocean sunset
Dinner at PRIME — Mauritian-French cuisine, seafood platter, duck confit, kids' menu
Breakfast at resort
Drive to Casela World of Adventures (45 min) — 250-hectare nature park with giant Aldabra tortoises (walk among them!), lions, giraffes, monkeys, bird aviary
Tortoise pen — sit with giant tortoises, feed them leaves, incredible for 5-year-old. Some are over 100 years old!
Safari bus ride through African animal enclosure — zebras, ostriches, antelopes, deer
Bird show and walk-through aviary — colorful parakeets, macaws, Mauritius kestrel (saved from extinction!)
Lunch at the park's restaurant — casual fare, burgers, salads, kids' options
Drive back, nap time at resort
Water sports at the resort — glass-bottom kayaking over coral, paddleboarding in the lagoon
Grandparents + toddlers: Guerlain Spa time or pool and beach lounging
Dinner at La Terrasse — Mauritian street food elevated — dholl puri, gateaux piments (chili cakes), rougaille, curry
Breakfast at resort

Undersea Walk at Grand Baie — walk on the ocean floor wearing a special helmet (no swimming needed!). See fish, coral, sea urchins up close. Available for adults and kids 7+ (grandparents can do it too!)
Kids at resort KidsOnly club (5-year-old) and beach with nanny/parent (toddlers)
Drive to Blue Bay Marine Park (30 min) — glass-bottom boat over pristine coral reef, sea turtles visible from the boat
Lunch at Chez Manuel on the coast — Mauritian Creole seafood, vindaye fish curry
Nap time at resort
Beach time in the lagoon — build sandcastles, spot sea cucumbers and baby reef fish in the shallows
Mauritian cooking class at the resort — learn to make fish vindaye and dholl puri as a family
Dinner at Tapasake overwater — romantic setting, Japanese tapas, sashimi, wagyu, kids' bento
Early breakfast — dolphins are best in the morning!
Dolphin swim excursion at Tamarin Bay — swim alongside spinner dolphins in the wild! 5-year-old in life vest with guide, toddlers watch from boat
Drive to Chamarel (30 min) — the Seven Colored Earth, a surreal geological formation of sand dunes in 7 colors, kids can see it from the viewing platform
Chamarel Waterfall viewpoint — 100-meter waterfall in lush jungle setting
Giant tortoise and animal enclosure at Chamarel — more tortoise time, plus Mauritian fruit bats in a walk-through enclosure
Lunch at Le Chamarel Restaurant — panoramic valley views, venison, local rum tasting for adults
Nap time — drive back to resort, rest
Catamaran sunset cruise from the resort — champagne, juice boxes, dolphins occasionally appear
BBQ dinner on the beach — Mauritian seafood grill, lantern-lit tables on the sand
Breakfast at resort
Boat to Île aux Aigrettes nature reserve (5 min from Mahébourg) — see the endangered pink pigeon, Telfair's skink (huge gecko), Aldabra tortoises, and restored native forest
Guided nature walk — conservation in action, learn how Mauritius saved species from extinction, spot rare birds
Lunch at Bois Chéri Tea Plantation restaurant — overlooking the tea fields, Mauritian-fusion cuisine
Tea factory tour — see how tea is processed from leaf to cup, taste different varieties, walk through the tea gardens
Nap time at resort
Spa afternoon — Guerlain spa for adults (alternate), kids play at pool and beach
Sega dance performance at the resort — traditional Mauritian music and dance, kids can join in the rhythm
Farewell dinner at PRIME — surf and turf, Mauritian rum cocktails, celebration dessert
Late breakfast at resort
Drive to Port Louis (45 min) — Central Market, vibrant colors, spices, tropical fruits, textiles, model ships (Mauritius specialty)
Caudan Waterfront — modern shopping area, Blue Penny Museum (see the rare Blue Penny stamp, one of the world's most valuable), craft market
Lunch at Lambic — Port Louis gastropub, Mauritian curry, craft beer, family-friendly
Drive back, nap time at resort
Final lagoon swim — soak up the last moments in the warm Indian Ocean
Family photo session on the peninsula — sunset with the Indian Ocean backdrop
Special farewell dinner — private dining on the beach, lanterns, personal chef, multi-course Mauritian feast
Pack for late-night departure
Depart Mauritius on Emirates overnight to Dubai, connect to LAX
Arrive Dubai, connect to LAX flight — Emirates lounge, showers, breakfast
Board Dubai → LAX flight (16 hrs) — kids sleep through first half, movies and activities for second half
Arrive LAX — the Mauritius adventure is complete!

tips: June-July is Mauritius's 'winter' but it's still 72-80°F — the dry season with less humidity, actually the most comfortable time to visit
The lagoon at Le Saint Géran is one of the safest swimming spots for toddlers — very shallow, warm, no waves
Mauritius was home to the dodo — the Natural History Museum in Port Louis has a dodo skeleton (5-year-old will love this)
The flight is long but Emirates Business Class makes it manageable — excellent kids' entertainment system
Mauritius is malaria-free — no special vaccinations needed
The rupee is the local currency but high-end places accept cards/USD
Model ship building is a Mauritian art form — makes a beautiful souvenir (shops at Caudan Waterfront)
Uber works well in Mauritius for independent day trips

name: Scottish Highlands & Isle of Skye
tagline: Castle stays, Highland cows, Loch Ness monster hunts & puffin islands — adventure in the misty Highlands
weather: 55–68°F, long daylight (18+ hrs!), mix of sun, clouds, and rain — pack layers and rain gear

hotel.name: Inverlochy Castle Hotel + Kinloch Lodge (Skye) + The Balmoral (Edinburgh)

rooms.name: Inverlochy Castle — Gate Lodge Suite (3 nights)
rooms.for: Adults + kids — spacious family suite in the grounds of the castle

rooms.name: Inverlochy Castle — Castle Room (3 nights)
rooms.for: Grandparents — in the main castle, views of Ben Nevis

rooms.name: Kinloch Lodge — Family Suite (2 nights)
rooms.for: All — historic hunting lodge on Skye, ground floor suite

rooms.name: The Balmoral, Edinburgh — Family Suite (2 nights)
rooms.for: Adults + kids — two-bedroom suite with views of Edinburgh Castle

highlights: Inverlochy Castle — Queen Victoria's favorite, beneath Ben Nevis
Kinloch Lodge — Michelin-star dining on the Isle of Skye
The Balmoral — Edinburgh's grand dame, J.K. Rowling finished Harry Potter here
Castle grounds for kids to run freely
Open fires, whisky libraries, afternoon tea traditions
Nannies can be arranged at each property

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on British Airways Club World to London Heathrow — overnight flight, kids sleep in flat beds
Arrive London Heathrow, connect to Inverness flight (1.5 hrs)
Arrive Inverness, pick up luxury SUV (Range Rover), drive to Inverlochy Castle Hotel (1.5 hrs through stunning Glen scenery)
Arrive Inverlochy Castle — check in, kids run on the castle lawn, mountain views
Afternoon tea in the Great Hall — scones, clotted cream, finger sandwiches, shortbread for kids
Dinner at the Castle's restaurant — Scottish salmon, venison, kids' menu available
Walk the castle grounds at dusk — 18+ hours of daylight means it's still bright! Look for red squirrels
Full Scottish breakfast at the castle — black pudding, haggis, tattie scones, eggs, beans
Drive to Loch Ness (1 hr) — scenic Great Glen route
Loch Ness boat cruise with Jacobite Cruises — sonar equipment shows the loch floor (exciting for 5-year-old monster hunter!)
Urquhart Castle ruins — atmospheric castle on the loch shore, short walk, incredible photo spot
Lunch at The Dores Inn — lochside pub, fish & chips, burgers, stunning Loch Ness views from the beer garden
Nap time — drive back to Inverlochy, toddlers sleep in car, rest at castle
Highland cow encounter at a local farm near Fort William — feed and pet the shaggy Highland coos!
Walk to the Nevis Range gondola — ride up Aonach Mor for panoramic mountain views (stroller OK)
Dinner at Inverlochy Castle — tasting menu with Scottish ingredients, kids' early sitting
Scottish breakfast at castle
Drive through Glen Coe (30 min) — the most dramatic valley in Scotland, stop at viewpoints, 5-year-old will think it's a dragon's lair
Glen Coe Visitor Centre — interactive exhibits about geology, wildlife, and the history of the massacre (kid-friendly level)
Easy walk to Signal Rock — flat forest trail, look for red deer and golden eagles overhead
Lunch at Clachaig Inn — legendary Highland pub, venison stew, fish pie
Nap time — drive back toward Fort William, rest at castle
Sea Life Sanctuary in Oban (detour, 40 min) — rescue seals, otters, touch pool, small aquarium with local marine life
Oban — walk the harbor, buy fresh seafood from the shacks on the pier
Dinner at Ee-Usk in Oban — waterfront restaurant, langoustines, halibut, crab
Drive back to Inverlochy (easy in the endless daylight)

days.title: Drive to Isle of Skye — Fairy Pools & Portree

schedule.activity: Final breakfast at Inverlochy Castle, check out
Scenic drive to Isle of Skye via Eilean Donan Castle (2.5 hrs) — the most photographed castle in Scotland, quick stop
Cross the Skye Bridge onto the island, lunch in Portree — The Granary cafe, seafood chowder, toasties
Nap time — drive to Kinloch Lodge (45 min south), check in, rest
Fairy Pools walk (short, easy) — crystal-clear turquoise pools beneath the Black Cuillin mountains, otherworldly. 5-year-old will believe in fairies
Back at Kinloch Lodge — settle in, explore the historic grounds, hot chocolate by the fire
Grandparents: whisky tasting in the library — fine selection of Talisker and other Scottish malts
Michelin-starred dinner at Kinloch Lodge — Chef Marcello Tully's Scottish-Asian fusion, local seafood, kids' menu available
Step outside — endless summer twilight over the Sound of Sleat
Breakfast at Kinloch Lodge — smoked salmon, porridge with heather honey
Wildlife boat trip from Elgol with Misty Isle Boat Trips — spot seals, sea eagles, porpoises, and occasionally minke whales along the dramatic coastline
Talisker Bay beach walk — dramatic black sand beach, waterfall onto the beach, sea stacks
Lunch at Talisker Distillery café (or pub in Carbost) — cullen skink (Scottish fish soup), sandwiches
Nap time at lodge
Drive to the Old Man of Storr viewpoint — iconic rock pinnacle, short walk to viewing area (stroller-manageable on lower path)
Portree harbor — colorful painted houses, ice cream from Over the Rainbow, watch fishing boats
Dinner at The Three Chimneys — one of Scotland's most celebrated restaurants, tasting menu with Skye langoustines, kids' portions available

days.title: Skye to Edinburgh — Highlands Road Trip

schedule.activity: Breakfast at Kinloch Lodge, check out
Drive south through the Highlands toward Edinburgh (5 hrs with stops) — stop at Loch Lomond viewpoint
Stop at Highland Wildlife Park near Kingussie — polar bears, Scottish wildcats, red pandas, wolves in natural enclosures. 5-year-old's favorite stop!
Lunch at The Cross in Kingussie — riverside restaurant, Scottish beef, homemade bread
Nap time in the car — toddlers sleep for the drive to Edinburgh (2.5 hrs)
Arrive Edinburgh, check into The Balmoral — grand entrance on Princes Street, views of Edinburgh Castle
Walk the Royal Mile — buskers, street performers, Edinburgh Castle looming above
Afternoon tea at The Balmoral's Palm Court — scones, cakes, kids' version with chocolate treats
Dinner at The Witchery by the Castle — Gothic candlelit restaurant beside Edinburgh Castle, Scottish game and seafood
Breakfast at the Balmoral
Edinburgh Castle — crown jewels, Mons Meg cannon, panoramic city views, Stone of Destiny. 5-year-old gets a 'Young Explorer' trail map
Dynamic Earth — interactive science museum about the planet, simulated volcano, ice cap walk, 4D movie. Brilliant for kids
Lunch at The Pantry in Stockbridge — beloved brunch spot, pancakes, eggs, kids' plates
Nap time at hotel
Edinburgh Zoo — pandas, penguins (penguin parade!), koalas, chimps. Stroller-friendly, beautiful hilltop setting
Calton Hill sunset walk — short climb, 360° views of the city, monuments, perfect family photo
Farewell dinner at The Lookout at Calton Hill — modern Scottish menu, panoramic glass walls, incredibly romantic and kid-welcoming
Walk back along Princes Street Gardens — kids run on the lawns beneath the castle
Early breakfast at hotel
Private transfer to Edinburgh Airport (20 min)
Fly EDI → London Heathrow (1.5 hrs)
Connect at Heathrow, BA lounge — relax before long flight
Depart LHR on British Airways Club World to LAX (11 hrs) — kids nap en route
Arrive LAX — Highland adventure complete!

tips: Scottish weather is unpredictable — pack rain jackets and layers even in summer, but the long daylight (18+ hours) is magical
Car seats are essential — book them with the rental car or bring your own
Midges (tiny biting insects) can be annoying June-July — bring Avon Skin So Soft (the Scottish secret weapon)
Scottish people are incredibly warm to children — expect friendly conversations everywhere
The Skye Bridge replaced the ferry — easy drive onto the island
Many restaurants in Scotland serve dinner early (5:30-6 PM) which is perfect for families
Edinburgh in late June may coincide with festivals — check Edinburgh International Film Festival dates

costBreakdown.hotel: $22,000 (mix of castle, lodge, and city hotel)

name: Monterey & Big Sur, California
tagline: World-class aquarium, rugged coastal beauty, and whale watching — all just a drive from home
weather: 55–68°F, classic coastal California; morning fog, afternoon sun; cool evenings
flightTime: 5–6 hr drive from LA (no flights needed)

outbound.route: Los Angeles → Monterey via US-101 or PCH

hotel.name: InterContinental The Clement Monterey
highlights: Steps from Monterey Bay Aquarium — literally next door
Ocean-view rooms overlooking Monterey Bay
Heated outdoor pool and fire pits
On-site restaurant with kid-friendly menu
Walking distance to Cannery Row shops and restaurants
Complimentary bike rentals
No flights needed — easy drive from LA

schedule.activity: Depart LA — take US-101 north (faster route, smoother for kids)
Rest stop in Santa Barbara — stretch legs, snack break, let kids run at a park
Lunch stop in San Luis Obispo — kid-friendly restaurant downtown
Continue north; toddlers nap in car seats
Arrive at InterContinental The Clement; check in and settle
Walk along Cannery Row — browse shops, enjoy the sea breeze, watch sea otters from the shore
Hotel pool time — warm up after the cool ocean air
Dinner on Cannery Row — fresh clam chowder in sourdough bowls
Kids to bed; adults enjoy fire pit on the hotel terrace
Breakfast at the hotel
Monterey Bay Aquarium — arrive at opening; start with the kelp forest and sea otters (5-year-old's mind will be blown)
Touch pools — kids can feel sea stars, urchins, and bat rays
Open Sea exhibit — giant ocean sunfish, sea turtles, jellyfish
Lunch at the aquarium café or step out to the hotel (steps away)
Nap break at hotel (literally next door) — kids rest, recharge
Return to aquarium — penguin feeding time, splash zone for toddlers, shark exhibit
Outdoor tide pool area — watch sea otters play in the wild from the aquarium deck
Depart aquarium; walk the coastal recreation trail
Dinner at a Cannery Row restaurant
Kids to bed
Breakfast
Whale watching from Monterey Bay — humpback whales, blue whales (summer peak), dolphins, sea lions; 3–4 hr tour
schedule.who: Adults + 5yr + Grandparents / 1 adult stays with toddlers

schedule.activity: Toddlers: Monterey State Beach for sand play and wading
Reunite at Fisherman's Wharf — lunch on the pier; watch sea lions bark below
Nap time at hotel
Dennis the Menace Playground in Monterey — fantastic playground designed by Hank Ketcham; perfect for the 5-year-old and 2.5-year-old
Walk through Pacific Grove — charming Victorian houses and monarch butterfly habitat
Dinner in Pacific Grove — Passionfish or another local gem
Kids to bed; adults stargaze from the hotel
Breakfast at hotel
17-Mile Drive — scenic coastal drive through Pebble Beach; stop at the Lone Cypress, Bird Rock (seals and sea lions), and Ghost Tree
Stop at a beach along 17-Mile Drive for sandcastle building and photos
Drive to Carmel-by-the-Sea — stroll the storybook village; browse galleries and shops
Lunch in Carmel — kid-friendly café
Carmel Beach — wide white sand beach; wading (cold water!) and sand play
Drive back to hotel; nap time for kids
Hotel pool and hot tub
Walk to the Monterey Bay Coastal Recreation Trail — sunset over the bay
Dinner at the hotel restaurant or Cannery Row
Kids to bed
Early breakfast — scenic drive day
Drive south to Big Sur via Highway 1 — one of America's most spectacular drives (1.5 hrs to Bixby Bridge)
Stop at Bixby Bridge for iconic photos
Point Lobos State Reserve — easy trails, tide pools, sea otters, harbor seals; the crown jewel of California state parks
Pfeiffer Beach — purple sand beach with the Keyhole Arch; short walk from parking lot
Lunch at Big Sur restaurant — Nepenthe for the view or Big Sur Deli for picnic supplies
Kids nap in the car on the drive back; or pull over at a viewpoint for a short rest
Return to Monterey; free time at hotel pool
Optional: revisit aquarium favorites (re-entry stamps from day 2) or beach time
Dinner on Cannery Row — try a new spot
Kids to bed; adults enjoy the fire pit
Leisurely late breakfast
Elkhorn Slough safari boat tour — kayak or pontoon; see hundreds of sea otters, harbor seals, pelicans, and more (calm water, great for 5-year-old)
schedule.who: Adults + 5yr + Grandparents / 1 adult + toddlers at beach

schedule.activity: Return from slough; head to Monterey State Beach for sand play
Lunch — fish tacos on the wharf
Nap time at hotel; pack bags
Final hotel pool time and Cannery Row stroll; buy souvenirs
Watch the sunset from Lovers Point in Pacific Grove
Farewell dinner — splurge at a top Monterey restaurant
Kids to bed; finish packing
Breakfast and checkout
Depart Monterey for home — take PCH (Highway 1) south through Big Sur for a different scenic route (if road is open)
Stop in San Simeon or Cambria — stretch break and quick lunch
Continue south; kids nap in car
Arrive home in LA
Rest and unpack; easy dinner at home
July 4th fireworks! — watch from a local spot or the backyard
Easy day at home — no jet lag! Unpack, do laundry, relax
Last vacation day — enjoy being home, look through photos, plan next trip

tips: No flights, no jet lag, no airports — the easiest trip on the entire list. Perfect for toddlers and grandparents.
Monterey Bay Aquarium is the best aquarium in the US — book tickets in advance; it sells out in summer.
The hotel is literally next door to the aquarium — you can pop back for naps and return later (keep tickets for re-entry).
Big Sur's Highway 1 is spectacular but winding — motion-sick kids should take medication beforehand.
Pack layers — Monterey is surprisingly cool (55–68°F) even in summer. Bring jackets for everyone.
Whale watching in Monterey Bay is world-class in summer — humpback and blue whales are regularly seen.
Dennis the Menace Playground is an underrated gem — the 5-year-old will not want to leave.
July 4th weekend will be busy — book restaurant reservations and aquarium tickets well in advance.
Two vehicles recommended for the group — or rent a large SUV/minivan.

costBreakdown.activities: $800–$1,200 (aquarium $60/adult, whale watching $60/adult, 17-Mile Drive $12/car, Elkhorn Slough tour, Point Lobos)
costBreakdown.food: $1,500–$2,500 (restaurant meals for 7 days)

name: Canary Islands (Tenerife + La Palma), Spain
tagline: Volcanic moonscapes, world-class whale watching, and Europe's best zoo — where Africa meets the Atlantic
weather: 72–82°F, sunny, warm, dry, perfect summer weather

hotel.name: The Ritz-Carlton Abama (Tenerife, 6 nights) + Hotel & Spa Hacienda de Abajo (La Palma, 1 night)

rooms.name: Ritz-Carlton Abama — Royal Suite
rooms.for: Grandparents — ocean view, private terrace, butler service

rooms.name: Ritz-Carlton Abama — Citadel Grand Suite (2BR)
rooms.for: Parents + 3 kids — connected bedrooms, kids' amenities provided

highlights: Ritz-Carlton Abama sits on its own private beach with year-round warm water
Two pools including a massive family lagoon pool and adults-only infinity pool
Ritz Kids program with stargazing, volcanic rock painting, and nature walks
Loro Parque (world's best zoo/aquarium) is 30 minutes away
La Palma is the world's first Starlight Reserve — unbelievable stargazing

schedule.activity: Arrive Tenerife South Airport, private transfer to Ritz-Carlton Abama (30 min)
Check in — welcome drinks, kids get Ritz Kids welcome pack, suite tour
Explore the resort — kids discover the lagoon pool, palm-tree lined paths, the private beach below
Beach time — Abama's private cove, warm Atlantic water, golden sand
Dinner at El Mirador — resort restaurant, Canarian tapas, grilled octopus, papas arrugadas (wrinkly potatoes with mojo sauce)
Kids bedtime — balmy evening air, ceiling fan, ocean sounds
Adults on the terrace — local Canarian wine, stars appearing over La Gomera island
Breakfast at the Ritz — full buffet with Spanish tortilla, tropical fruits, pastries
Drive to Loro Parque in Puerto de la Cruz (45 min) — voted world's best zoo multiple times
Loro Parque — shark tunnel (the 5-year-old loses their mind), orca show, penguin exhibit (largest in Southern Europe), gorillas, tigers
Lunch inside Loro Parque — multiple restaurants, kids eat while watching flamingos
Nap break — find a shady bench, baby in stroller, or toddlers nap in car for a bit
Continue Loro Parque — dolphin show, parrot show, aquarium (one of Europe's largest), jungle exhibit
Drive back to Ritz-Carlton, explore Puerto de la Cruz's old town briefly en route — colorful streets, ice cream
Pool time at the resort — decompress after a big day
Dinner at Kabuki — Ritz-Carlton's Michelin-starred Japanese restaurant (reserve ahead)
Evening walk along the resort's cliff path — sunset over La Gomera
Breakfast at the resort
Whale and dolphin watching boat trip from Los Gigantes marina (20 min from resort) — pilot whales and bottlenose dolphins are year-round residents
Los Gigantes cliffs viewpoint — 800m vertical walls plunging into the sea, spectacular from the boat and land
Lunch at El Rincón de Juan Carlos in Los Gigantes — 2 Michelin stars, but relaxed (tasting menu or à la carte)
Nap time — drive back, kids sleep in car, or rest at resort
Abama Beach afternoon — swimming, sandcastles, snorkeling in the calm cove
Ritz Kids program — volcanic rock painting workshop, the 5-year-old makes art with lava stones
Spa time for grandparents — Canarian volcanic stone massage at the Ritz spa
Dinner at Txoko — Basque-Canarian fusion at the resort, pintxos, grilled meats
Breakfast at resort
Drive to Teide National Park (1 hr) — Spain's highest peak, lunar landscapes, the 5-year-old thinks you're on another planet
Cable car to 3,555m elevation (optional — grandparents may prefer the lower viewpoints) — panoramic views of all the Canary Islands
Grandparents and toddlers explore Roques de García — dramatic rock formations with easy, flat paths
Picnic lunch in the caldera — pre-packed from the Ritz, eat surrounded by volcanic moonscape
Drive back, nap time for toddlers in car
Pool and beach afternoon at the resort — easy day after altitude
Visit Abama Golf resort gardens — peacocks roaming, subtropical plants, gentle stroller walk
Dinner at Melvin by Martín Berasategui — Ritz-Carlton's other Michelin restaurant, Basque-Canarian seafood
Return to Teide for stargazing (optional — Teide is Europe's top stargazing spot, guided tour available)
Lazy breakfast — sleep in today
Siam Park — voted world's best water park, Thai-themed, massive wave pool, lazy river, kids' zone
Book a VIP cabana at Siam Park — shade, fridge, private area, essential with toddlers
Lunch at the park — Thai food stalls, pizza, burgers, eat in your cabana
Nap time — toddlers nap in the shaded cabana while the 5-year-old splashes in the kids' area
More water park fun — the Tower of Power slide goes through a shark tank (the 5-year-old: 'AGAIN!')
Head back to Ritz-Carlton — showers, rest
Sunset at Abama Beach — one of the best golden hours of the trip
Dinner at La Vieja — local Canarian restaurant in nearby Alcalá, family-run, amazing fresh fish

days.title: Day Trip to La Palma — Starlight Island

schedule.activity: Early breakfast at resort
Quick flight from Tenerife North to La Palma (30 min) — or fast ferry (2.5 hrs)
Drive through La Palma's laurel forests — UNESCO site, ancient trees dripping with moss, magical for kids
Visit the Caldera de Taburiente National Park — volcanic crater viewpoint, short walks among Canarian pines
Lunch in Santa Cruz de La Palma — charming colonial town, seafood at La Placeta
Nap at Hacienda de Abajo — check in to this beautiful colonial hotel, rest
Walk through banana plantations — La Palma grows Europe's bananas, the 5-year-old picks one from the tree
Natural sea pools of La Fajana — volcanic rock pools filled by the Atlantic, warm and calm for kids
Dinner at Hacienda de Abajo — garden dining, Palmero specialties
Stargazing from the Roque de los Muchachos viewpoint (2,400m) — La Palma is the world's first Starlight Reserve, the Milky Way is overwhelming
Breakfast at Hacienda de Abajo, check out
Fly or ferry back to Tenerife
Lunch at Ritz-Carlton — back to base, familiar comfort
Nap time at resort — extended rest after the La Palma adventure
Scenic drive to Masca village (1 hr) — one of the most beautiful mountain villages in the Canaries, perched on a cliff
Walk through Masca — dramatic ravine, old stone houses, coffee at the village café
Drive back via the coast — stop at Playa de la Arena for a quick volcanic black sand beach visit
Farewell dinner at Abama Beach Club — feet in the sand, grilled seafood, Canarian wine, sunset
Pack up, final Canarian evening
Final breakfast at Ritz-Carlton — savor those papas arrugadas one last time
Check out, transfer to Tenerife South Airport
Flight to Madrid (2.5 hrs)
Madrid layover — quick taxi to Retiro Park, row boats on the lake (kids love the ducks), Palacio de Cristal
Churros and chocolate at Chocolatería San Ginés — the most famous in Madrid, open since 1894
Back to Madrid Barajas Airport
Depart Madrid for LAX (11.5 hrs)
Arrive LAX late evening (time zones work in your favor westbound)
Private car home — incredible trip complete

tips: The Canary Islands have PERFECT weather year-round — June is warm, sunny, and dry
Loro Parque is worth a full day — it's genuinely one of the world's best zoos/aquariums
Siam Park + Loro Parque combo tickets save money — get them online in advance
The Ritz-Carlton Abama is one of Spain's best family resorts — the kids club is excellent
Rent a car — you'll want flexibility to explore Tenerife's diverse landscapes
Teide National Park requires a free permit for the summit — book online weeks ahead
Canarian food is incredible and cheap — papas arrugadas, mojo sauces, fresh fish
La Palma stargazing is truly world-class — it rivals Chilean desert observatories

name: Maldives
tagline: Baby reef sharks in the shallows, bioluminescent beaches after dark, and overwater villas above a turquoise lagoon
weather: 82–88°F, wet season (but less crowded, short rain bursts, mostly sunny), warm ocean

outbound.route: LAX → Dubai (DXB) → Malé (MLE), then seaplane to resort

hotel.name: Soneva Fushi (Baa Atoll)

rooms.name: Soneva Fushi — 2-Bedroom Crusoe Villa with Pool
rooms.for: Grandparents — beachfront, private pool, butler service

rooms.name: Soneva Fushi — 3-Bedroom Beach Retreat with Pool & Slide
rooms.for: Parents + 3 kids — waterslide from villa to pool, massive space

highlights: Soneva Fushi pioneered luxury eco-tourism in the Maldives — no shoes, no news, no formality
The 3BR villa has a WATERSLIDE into the pool — the 5-year-old won't want to leave
The Den kids club is Maldives' best — pirate ship, Lego room, marine biology lab, cinema
Resident marine biologist leads daily reef discovery for kids
Shoes-off, barefoot luxury — perfect for toddlers who hate shoes anyway
Private observatory with one of Asia's most powerful telescopes

schedule.activity: Arrive Malé, fast-tracked through immigration (Soneva arranges)
Scenic seaplane transfer to Soneva Fushi (35 min) — flying over atolls at sunset, the kids press faces to windows
Arrive Soneva Fushi — barefoot welcome on the jetty, cold towels, coconut water, personal Mr./Ms. Friday butler greets you
Buggy ride to your beach retreat villa — jaws drop at the private pool with waterslide
Welcome dinner on your villa deck — chef-prepared, lantern-lit, feet in the sand, waves lapping
Kids bedtime — the sound of the ocean is the best white noise machine
Adults walk the beach — check for bioluminescent plankton in the shallows (June is good season!)

days.title: Shark Nursery & The Den Kids Club

schedule.activity: Breakfast at Out of the Blue — eggs, tropical fruits, smoothie bowls, pastries, ocean views
Baby shark nursery visit with the marine biologist — wade in the shallows where baby blacktip reef sharks swim (they're 18 inches long and harmless!) — the 5-year-old's DREAM
The Den kids club tour — pirate ship playground, Lego room, giant chess, outdoor cinema, marine discovery center
Lunch at Fresh in the Garden — organic restaurant in Soneva's own vegetable garden, farm-to-table
Nap time — villa time, pool, the 5-year-old uses the waterslide approximately 400 times
Guided snorkel on the house reef — turtles, reef sharks, Nemo fish, incredible coral (Baa Atoll is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve)
Grandparents with toddlers — villa pool and beach time
Sunset dolphin cruise — spinner dolphins leap beside the boat, champagne for adults, juice boxes for kids
Dinner at Mihiree Mithaa — Maldivian cuisine, fish curry, roshi flatbread, coconut dishes
Observatory night — look at Saturn's rings, Jupiter's moons through the resort's telescope
Breakfast at Out of the Blue
Manta ray snorkeling trip to Hanifaru Bay (Baa Atoll's famous manta aggregation site, June is peak season!) — swim with manta rays up to 15ft across
Grandparents and toddlers at The Den — sandcastle building, baby pool, marine art projects
Castaway picnic on a deserted sandbank — butler sets up a private lunch on a tiny island in the middle of the ocean
Nap time — back to villa, afternoon rest with ocean breeze through the open-air bathroom
Soneva chocolate and ice cream room — make your own chocolate, unlimited artisan ice cream flavors (yes, it's free and open 24/7)
Stand-up paddleboarding in the lagoon — glass-clear water, spot stingrays and baby sharks below you
Sunset from the overwater deck — cocktails, juice, the sky turns every color
Dinner at So Engaging — Soneva's Japanese-Peruvian fusion, the tuna sashimi was swimming hours ago
Breakfast at the villa — butler delivers the full spread to your deck
Guided turtle snorkel — Soneva's house reef has resident hawksbill and green sea turtles
Marine biology session at The Den — the 5-year-old learns about coral reef ecosystems, gets a 'Junior Marine Biologist' certificate
Lunch at Out of the Blue — pizza, pasta, grilled fish
Nap time — full villa beach day afternoon
Beach and pool time — snorkeling, waterslide, sandcastles, coconut drinks
Soneva spa — couple's massage for parents while grandparents play with kids in the pool
Sunset fishing trip — traditional Maldivian handline fishing, the 5-year-old catches a grouper
The chef cooks your catch at the villa — private BBQ dinner on the beach
Bioluminescent beach walk — drag your feet through the shallow water and it glows blue
Breakfast at Out of the Blue
Visit a local Maldivian island — see how locals live, visit the tiny school, buy fresh tuna at the harbor
Coral reef planting with Soneva's marine team — the family plants a coral frame that Soneva nurtures for years (you get photo updates!)
Lunch at Fresh in the Garden
Nap time at villa
The Den afternoon — outdoor cinema under the stars for kids, popcorn and movie
Adults relax — read, spa, pool, hammock
Glass-blowing demonstration at Soneva — the island has its own glass studio, kids watch artisans at work
Dinner at So Wild — Soneva's newest restaurant, ocean-to-plate concept
Movie under the stars at Cinema Paradiso — outdoor screen on the beach, family-friendly film
Sleep in, lazy breakfast delivered to villa
Semi-submarine ride — see the reef without getting wet, perfect for grandparents and toddlers who can't snorkel
Lunch at Shades — poolside, light bites, sushi
Nap time — this is the lazy day you all need mid-trip
Soneva's waste-to-wealth tour — see how the resort recycles everything, glass studio uses recycled glass, eco-education for the 5-year-old
Villa time — waterslide, pool, beach, the toddlers dig in the sand for hours
Final sunset — the family gathers on the beach, champagne and juice, photos
Farewell dinner at Out of the Blue — lobster, crab, the finest meal of the trip
Final observatory session — one last look at the southern sky
Final villa breakfast — soak in every last moment
Check out and seaplane to Malé (35 min) — aerial views of the atolls one last time
Emirates flight Malé to Dubai (4.5 hrs)
Dubai layover — check into One&Only Royal Mirage for the night
Dubai Aquarium & Underwater Zoo at Dubai Mall — massive shark tank (the 5-year-old screams with joy), glass-bottom boat, penguins
Dubai Fountain show — the world's largest choreographed fountain, runs every 30 min after sunset
Dinner at Pierchic — over-the-water restaurant, seafood, Arabian Gulf sunset
Early bed — flight home tomorrow
Breakfast at hotel
Transfer to Dubai International Airport
Depart Dubai for LAX on Emirates (16 hrs)
Arrive LAX (same day — time zone magic)
Recovery day at home — unpack, laundry, relive memories, start planning the return trip

tips: June is 'wet season' but in practice means 1-2 short rain bursts per day — still mostly sunny and MUCH fewer tourists
Soneva Fushi is barefoot — no shoes anywhere on the island, which toddlers LOVE
The baby shark nursery is one of the most incredible wildlife experiences for a shark-obsessed kid
Seaplane transfers only operate in daylight — plan arrivals/departures accordingly
Hanifaru Bay manta season peaks June–November — you're in prime time
The chocolate and ice cream room is open 24/7 and is included — dangerous for adults
Book the 3BR villa with slide WELL in advance — it's the most requested room at Soneva
Bring reef-safe sunscreen — the Maldives' reefs are fragile and recovering

name: Jordan (Dead Sea, Wadi Rum & Aqaba)
tagline: Float in the Dead Sea, ride camels through Mars-like deserts & snorkel with sharks in the Red Sea
weather: 85–105°F, very hot and dry — Dead Sea and Aqaba are hottest, desert nights cool to 70°F

outbound.duration: 15.5h (one stop, typically via ORD or DOH)

hotel.name: Kempinski Hotel Ishtar Dead Sea + Sun City Camp (Wadi Rum) + Hyatt Regency Aqaba Ayla

rooms.name: Kempinski — Royal Villa with private pool (3 nights)
rooms.for: Adults + kids — 2-bedroom villa, private infinity pool overlooking Dead Sea

rooms.name: Kempinski — Deluxe Sea View Room (3 nights)
Sun City Camp — Martian Dome Tent (1 night)
rooms.for: All — luxury desert domes with AC, ensuite, telescope

rooms.name: Hyatt Regency Aqaba — Family Suite (3 nights)
rooms.for: Adults + kids — sea view, connecting rooms

rooms.name: Hyatt Regency Aqaba — Deluxe King (3 nights)

highlights: Kempinski's 9 pools cascade down to the Dead Sea shore
Dead Sea mud spa — therapeutic for grandparents' joints
Sun City Camp's Martian domes look like a movie set
Aqaba's coral reef is uncrowded and pristine
Jordanian hospitality is legendary — extremely family-friendly culture
Private guides available throughout

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on Royal Jordanian Crown Class
Arrive Amman, private transfer to Kempinski Dead Sea (45 min)
Check in, settle into villa, kids explore the private pool
First Dead Sea float — the lowest point on Earth! Even the 5-year-old will float effortlessly (keep water away from eyes for toddlers)
Rinse off, Dead Sea mud application — coat yourselves in mineral mud, great photo op
Dinner at Kempinski's Obelisk Restaurant — Jordanian mezze feast: hummus, falafel, grilled lamb, kids' favorites
Kids bedtime, adults enjoy Dead Sea sunset from terrace
Breakfast at Kempinski — Arabic breakfast spread with labneh, za'atar, fresh bread, eggs
Grandparents: Anantara Spa morning — Dead Sea mineral treatments, therapeutic for joints. Parents + kids: pool time at the cascading pools
Drive to Amman (45 min) — visit the Citadel with Roman ruins, panoramic views over the ancient city
Lunch at Hashem Restaurant downtown — legendary falafel and hummus, beloved by locals, ultra-cheap and delicious
Nap time — drive back to Dead Sea, rest at resort
Private beach time at Kempinski — float again, play in the pools, build salt crystal castles on the shore
Mosaic art workshop at the resort — make a small mosaic (Jordanian tradition) as a family
Dinner at Al-Saraya — Jordanian grill restaurant, mansaf (national dish — lamb in yogurt), mixed grills
Early breakfast and departure — drive to Petra (3 hrs via King's Highway, scenic route)
Enter Petra through the Siq — narrow canyon with towering walls, 5-year-old will feel like Indiana Jones. Strollers possible on main path
The Treasury (Al-Khazneh) — the iconic carved facade, jaw-dropping for everyone. Camel rides available for kids!
Walk the Street of Facades — more carved tombs, donkey rides for tired little legs
Lunch at The Basin Restaurant inside Petra — buffet with local dishes, shaded terrace, good kids' options
Head back through the Siq — toddlers in carrier/stroller, hire a horse cart for grandparents if needed
Nap time in the car — drive toward Wadi Rum (1.5 hrs)
Arrive Wadi Rum desert — check into Sun City Camp's Martian Dome tents, marvel at the red desert landscape
Sunset 4x4 Jeep tour of Wadi Rum — Lawrence's Spring, rock bridges, Bedouin petroglyphs. Kids ride in the open-top jeep
Traditional Bedouin zarb dinner — lamb and vegetables cooked underground in the sand, eaten under the stars
Stargazing in the desert — Wadi Rum has some of the clearest skies on Earth, camp provides telescope

days.title: Wadi Rum to Aqaba — Red Sea

schedule.activity: Sunrise over the desert — magical light on red sandstone (step outside the dome)
Bedouin breakfast — flatbread baked on coals, eggs, tea with sage
Camel ride through the desert (30 min) — gentle camels, 5-year-old rides with a guide, toddlers in parent's lap
Drive to Aqaba (1 hr) — Jordan's Red Sea coast
Check into Hyatt Regency Aqaba, settle in, resort pool time
Lunch at AlBaba Restaurant in Aqaba — fresh Red Sea fish, mezze
Nap time at resort
Glass-bottom boat ride in the Red Sea — coral reefs visible from the boat, tropical fish, safe for all ages
Aqaba Fort & flagpole — the iconic site from Lawrence of Arabia, quick visit
Dinner at Rovers Return on the Aqaba corniche — seafood, waterfront, casual vibe
Breakfast at hotel
Private snorkeling boat trip to Aqaba Marine Park — blacktip reef sharks (harmless!), sea turtles, lionfish, colorful coral. 5-year-old's shark dream comes true!
Grandparents + toddlers: resort pool and beach, kids' splash area
Family beach time at South Beach Aqaba — calm, warm, shallow water
Lunch at Red Sea Grill — grilled shrimp, calamari, fresh juices
Nap time at resort
Aqaba Aquarium (Marine Science Station) — small but excellent, Red Sea species, touch tanks
Resort pool and beach afternoon — relax and play
Dinner at Burj Al Hamam at Hyatt — upscale Lebanese cuisine, shisha for adults on the terrace
Aqaba promenade walk — ice cream, warm evening breeze
Late breakfast at hotel
Aqaba Bird Observatory — surprising bird diversity at this Red Sea wetland, flamingos, herons, bee-eaters. Easy walk on boardwalks
Beach time — Berenice Beach Club for a more upscale beach experience, loungers, food service
Lunch at Syrian Palace Restaurant — Syrian and Jordanian cuisine, shawarma, fattoush salad
Nap time at resort
Souk shopping in Aqaba — spices, hand-painted ceramics, Bedouin silver jewelry, miniature camels for the 5-year-old
Traditional hammam experience at the resort spa — adults alternate, kids play at pool
Farewell dinner at Captain's Restaurant — Aqaba's finest seafood, sunset over Saudi Arabia visible across the gulf
Early breakfast, check out
Drive from Aqaba to Amman (3.5 hrs on the Desert Highway, or domestic flight 1 hr if preferred)
Stop at Dana Nature Reserve overlook — dramatic rift valley views, quick stretch
Lunch in Madaba (if driving) — Haret Jdoudna restaurant in a 100-year-old house, see the famous Byzantine mosaics at St. George's Church
Arrive Amman, check into Four Seasons Amman for final night, nap time
Rainbow Street walk in Amman — hip neighborhood, street art, cafés, Souk Jara market (if Friday)
Jordan Museum — Dead Sea Scrolls, ancient artifacts, kid-friendly interactive exhibits
Final dinner at Sufra Restaurant in Amman — elegant Jordanian home, traditional feast on the terrace
Pack for very early departure
Transfer to Queen Alia International Airport (30 min)
Depart Amman on Royal Jordanian — overnight flight, everyone sleeps
Arrive LAX — an unforgettable Jordanian adventure complete

tips: Jordan is extremely safe for tourists — one of the safest countries in the Middle East
The Dead Sea is drying up — visit while you can, and don't let toddlers drink the water (10x saltier than ocean)
Petra requires a lot of walking — bring a carrier for toddlers and consider hiring a horse/donkey cart for grandparents
Wadi Rum can be very hot in late June — schedule outdoor activities for early morning or late afternoon
Jordanian people adore children — expect locals to be incredibly warm and generous
The Jordan Pass ($99 adult) covers Petra admission and visa fee — excellent value
Keep kids hydrated — carry water everywhere in summer. Electrolyte packets recommended
The Aqaba reef sharks are small blacktip reef sharks — completely harmless and thrilling for kids

costBreakdown.hotel: $16,500 (mix of Dead Sea, desert camp, Aqaba, Amman)

name: Copenhagen, Denmark
tagline: Tivoli magic, Lego wonderland, and the world's happiest city with kids
weather: 55–72°F, long daylight hours, occasional rain showers

hotel.name: Nimb Hotel
highlights: Located inside Tivoli Gardens — unlimited park access included
Michelin-star dining at Nimb Brasserie
Rooftop terrace overlooking the amusement park
Babysitting service available
Walking distance to Strøget shopping and canal tours

schedule.activity: Arrive at Copenhagen Airport — private van transfer to Nimb Hotel (20 min)
Check in and freshen up
Light lunch at Nimb Bar'n'Grill — burgers, salads, kids' menu
Nap time — long travel day, everyone rests
First gentle exploration of Tivoli Gardens — the Petzi ride (toddler-friendly), carousel, gardens
Dinner at Grøften in Tivoli — traditional Danish open-faced sandwiches (smørrebrød)
Tivoli illuminations — see the park light up (9 PM sunset so still bright!)
Bedtime for little ones at hotel — grandparents watch from balcony
Breakfast at Nimb — Danish pastries, fresh juice, eggs
Canal boat tour (1 hr) from Nyhavn — covered boat, see Little Mermaid, Christiansborg Palace, opera house. Kids love the low bridges!
Walk along Nyhavn — colorful houses photo ops, ice cream at Vaffelbageren
Lunch at Told & Snaps in Nyhavn — herring for grandparents, chicken for kids
Nap time back at hotel (10 min taxi ride)
The Round Tower (Rundetårn) — gentle spiral ramp to the top (stroller-friendly!), views of Copenhagen
Strøget pedestrian street — window shopping, street performers, LEGO Store flagship
Dinner at Restaurant Schønnemann — Copenhagen's oldest smørrebrød restaurant
Early breakfast at hotel
Private van to Legoland Billund (2.5 hrs) — kids watch movies, toddlers nap en route
Legoland Billund — Miniland (tiny LEGO cities!), Duplo Land for toddlers, LEGO Ninjago ride for 5 yr old
Lunch inside the park — Knight's BBQ restaurant
Nap break — find a shaded bench near Duplo Land, baby sleeps in stroller, 2.5 yr old rests
More rides — Pirate Splash, LEGOREDO canoe, Fairy Tale Brook (all toddler-friendly)
LEGO Store for souvenirs — Pick-a-Brick wall
Drive back to Copenhagen (2.5 hrs) — kids sleep all the way
Simple dinner at hotel — room service or Nimb Bar
Breakfast at Nimb
Taxi to The Blue Planet (Den Blå Planet) — Northern Europe's largest aquarium (20 min)
Explore the aquarium — ocean tunnel walk-through, touch pools (sea stars, hermit crabs), tropical rainforest section, sea otters
Lunch at the aquarium café
Nap time — taxi back to hotel
Grandparents explore the King's Garden (Kongens Have) — peaceful, benches, puppet theater in summer
Tivoli Gardens afternoon — more rides, the Bumper Cars, the Dragon rollercoaster (gentle, 5 yr old loves it)
Danish pastry tasting tour — hotel concierge arranges a guided walk to 3 top bakeries
Dinner at Höst — Nordic cuisine in a cozy farmhouse-style interior, excellent kids' portions
Breakfast at Nimb
Private van to Frederiksborg Castle (45 min) — Denmark's Versailles, on a lake, gorgeous gardens
Explore the castle — knights' hall, chapel, baroque gardens. 5 yr old gets a children's activity booklet. Gardens are flat and stroller-friendly
Lunch at Slotsherrens Kro — charming inn near the castle
Nap time in the van — drive to Bakken amusement park (30 min)
Bakken (Dyrehavsbakken) — the world's oldest amusement park, free entry, gentle rides, deer in the surrounding forest
Deer spotting in Dyrehaven forest — hundreds of free-roaming deer, magical for kids
Drive back to hotel
Dinner at Tivoli Food Hall — everyone picks what they want
Leisurely breakfast
Rosenborg Castle — see the crown jewels (5 yr old fascinated), suits of armor, then play in King's Garden playground
Torvehallerne food market — gourmet stalls, fresh smoothies, chocolate, Danish hot dogs
Lunch grazing at the market — pick and choose from stalls
Nap time at hotel
Superkilen Park — the colorful playground with swings and slides from around the world
Spa or rest at hotel
Sunset bike ride along the harbor (cargo bike with kid seats, very Danish!)
Special dinner at Nimb Brasserie — dress up night, tasting menu with kids' adaptation
Breakfast at Nimb
Visit The Little Mermaid statue at Langelinie — quick stop, photo op, harbor walk
Kastellet fortress — star-shaped fortress with moat, ducks, windmill, flat easy walk
Lunch at Amass — waterfront restaurant with garden, relaxed atmosphere
Nap time at hotel
Amager Strandpark beach — sandy beach, lagoon with calm shallow water perfect for wading, playgrounds
Ice cream at Ismageriet
Farewell dinner at Geranium — 3-star Michelin (adults alternate, grandparents babysit) OR family dinner at Madklubben
Final breakfast at Nimb — savor those pastries
Last Tivoli visit — one more carousel ride, photos by the lake
Souvenir shopping — Royal Copenhagen porcelain, LEGO sets, Danish design toys
Lunch at Paludan Bogcafé — bookshop café, cozy goodbye meal
Nap time and packing at hotel
Final walk through Tivoli Gardens — sunset light
Early dinner at hotel
Early bedtime — travel day tomorrow
Breakfast and check out
Private transfer to Copenhagen Airport (20 min)
Flight CPH → LAX
Arrive at LAX

tips: Get the Copenhagen Card — covers transit, Tivoli, museums, canal tour, and more
Danish restaurants are very child-friendly — highchairs and kids' menus everywhere
Rain gear is essential — light waterproof jackets for everyone, rain cover for stroller
Cargo bikes with kid seats can be rented — the most Copenhagen experience possible
Book Legoland tickets online in advance for a discount and skip-the-line entry
The metro runs 24/7 and is stroller-friendly with elevators at every station

name: Tasmania, Australia
tagline: Wombats waddling at dusk, Tasmanian devils screaming at night, and pristine wilderness at the bottom of the world
weather: 40–55°F, Tasmanian winter (but mild), rain likely, crisp and beautiful

hotel.name: Saffire Freycinet (5 nights) + MACq 01 Hotel Hobart (2 nights)

rooms.name: Saffire — Luxury Suite
rooms.for: Grandparents — panoramic Hazards views, ground level

rooms.name: Saffire — Private Pavilion
rooms.for: Parents + 3 kids — separate living area, extra space

highlights: Saffire Freycinet is all-inclusive — meals, wine, guided experiences included
Private guided wildlife encounters with resident wombats at dusk
Floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the Hazards mountains and Great Oyster Bay
In-house spa using native Tasmanian botanicals
MACq 01 is Hobart's most unique hotel — each room tells a Tasmanian story

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on Qantas overnight flight to Sydney — kids sleep on the plane
Business class lie-flat beds, kids' amenity kits, movies
Arrive Hobart, private transfer to MACq 01 Hotel on the waterfront (20 min)
Check in, freshen up, light lunch at the hotel's Evolve Dining
Nap time — everyone rests after the long haul, jet lag recovery
Walk along Hobart waterfront to Salamanca Place — galleries, buskers, the 5-year-old explores
Tasmanian Museum and Art Gallery — free entry, excellent natural history section with Tasmanian tiger exhibit
Dinner at Templo — modern Tasmanian cuisine, locally foraged ingredients
Walk back to hotel, hot chocolate at Salamanca, early bedtime for jet lag
Breakfast at MACq 01 — Tasmanian smoked salmon, eggs, proper flat white coffee
Ferry to MONA (Museum of Old and New Art) — the ferry ride itself is an experience, harbor views
Explore MONA — underground museum built into sandstone cliffs, kids love the weird and wonderful installations
Lunch at MONA's Faro restaurant — stunning vineyard views, Tasmanian wine for adults
Ferry back to Hobart, nap time in car en route to Bonorong
Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary — hand-feed kangaroos, hold a quoll, see Tasmanian devils up close, wombat encounters
Private after-hours feeding tour — watch Tasmanian devils eat (they scream — the 5-year-old will be thrilled and terrified)
Dinner at Peacock and Jones in Salamanca — wood-fired meats, veggie sides, family-friendly
Pack for tomorrow's move to Saffire Freycinet
Breakfast and check out of MACq 01
Scenic drive to Freycinet Peninsula (2.5 hrs, stop at historic Ross Village for scones)
Arrive Saffire Freycinet — jaw-dropping arrival as the Hazards mountains appear through the glass lobby
Welcome lunch at Palate Restaurant — all-inclusive, Tasmanian oysters, wallaby carpaccio
Nap time — settle into suites, toddlers rest, the views are hypnotic
Guided beach walk on Coles Bay — white sand, turquoise water, look for sea eagles overhead
Complimentary Tasmanian wine and cheese tasting at the Saffire lounge
Dinner at Palate — seven-course tasting menu with Tasmanian produce
Night wildlife spotting walk — Saffire guide takes you to see wild wombats, possums, and pademelons on the property
Breakfast at Palate — eggs Benedict with Tasmanian smoked trout
Guided Wineglass Bay lookout walk (1.5 hrs return, moderate but doable with baby in carrier, grandparent-friendly pace)
Return to Saffire for lunch — kids eat, adults sip Tasmanian sparkling wine
Nap time at the lodge
Saffire's complimentary oyster farm experience — boat to Freycinet Marine Farm, shuck and eat oysters straight from the water
Spa time — native Tasmanian pepperberry body treatment for grandparents
Kids play on the lodge grounds — spot wallabies grazing on the lawns
Dinner at Palate — Chef's table experience tonight
Stargazing — Tasmania has some of Australia's darkest skies
Breakfast at Saffire
Drive to Bicheno (30 min) — penguin capital of Tasmania
Bicheno's East Coast Natureworld — hand-feed kangaroos, see wombats, Tasmanian devils, koalas
Lunch at the Gulch Fish Café in Bicheno — famous fish and chips, harbor views
Nap time — drive back to Saffire, toddlers sleep
Kayaking in Coles Bay (guided, calm waters) — spot sea eagles and dolphins
Grandparents relax at Saffire — reading by the fire, tea and scones
Drive back to Bicheno for the evening penguin tour — watch Little Penguins waddle ashore at dusk
Dinner at Saffire — fireplace lit, cozy winter evening dining
Early breakfast at Saffire — big day ahead
Scenic drive to Cradle Mountain (3 hrs via Midlands, stop in Campbell Town for pastries)
Arrive Cradle Mountain — Dove Lake circuit walk (easy 6km boardwalk around the lake, stroller-friendly sections)
Lunch at Peppers Cradle Mountain Lodge — fireside dining, warming soups
Nap time — kids rest in the car, drive back partway
Devils@Cradle sanctuary — close-up Tasmanian devil encounters, quoll feeding, conservation talk
Enchanted Walk through the rainforest — platypus spotting at the creek (dusk is best), ferns, moss, magical
Drive back to Saffire (3 hrs, kids sleep, adults enjoy the golden light on empty roads)
Late supper at Saffire — the kitchen keeps something warm for you

days.title: Last Saffire Morning & Drive to Launceston

schedule.activity: Sleep in, lazy final breakfast at Saffire — soak in the views one last time
Final wildlife walk — say goodbye to the resident wombats and wallabies
Check out of Saffire and drive to Launceston (2 hrs)
Arrive Launceston, lunch at Stillwater restaurant — one of Tasmania's best, in a heritage flour mill
Nap time at the hotel or in strollers at Cataract Gorge
Cataract Gorge — chairlift ride over the gorge (kids love it), peacocks roaming, swimming pool, suspension bridge
Explore Launceston's old town — heritage buildings, Harvest Market area
Farewell dinner at Black Cow Bistro — famous Tasmanian steak restaurant, best beef in Australia
Pack, early bedtime for morning flight
Early wake-up, quick breakfast
Depart Launceston for Melbourne (1hr flight), connect to LAX
Arrive LAX same calendar day (date line magic)

tips: It's WINTER in Tasmania in June/July — pack warm layers, rain jackets, and beanies for everyone
Despite being winter, wildlife is incredibly active — wombats and devils are easier to spot
Saffire Freycinet is all-inclusive — no surprise bills for food, wine, or most activities
Car seats: pre-arrange with rental company or bring your own (Australian law requires them)
Tasmanian devil feeding times at sanctuaries are unmissable — they eat with terrifying intensity
The 5-year-old will talk about hand-feeding kangaroos for years
Roads are winding — allow extra time and bring motion sickness remedies for kids

name: Croatia (Dubrovnik + Islands)
tagline: Ancient walled cities, island-hopping by private yacht, blue caves, and the clearest water in the Mediterranean
weather: 75–88°F, sunny, warm sea, dry, perfect Mediterranean summer

hotel.name: Villa Dubrovnik (3 nights) + Private Yacht (2 nights) + Lesic Dimitri Palace, Korčula (2 nights)

rooms.name: Villa Dubrovnik — Grand Suite Sea View
rooms.for: Grandparents — private terrace, elevator to sea-level beach

rooms.name: Villa Dubrovnik — Two-Bedroom Suite
rooms.for: Parents + 3 kids — connecting rooms, balcony over Adriatic

rooms.name: Private Yacht — 3-cabin motor yacht with crew

highlights: Villa Dubrovnik has a cliff-side elevator to a private swimming pier on the Adriatic
2 nights on a private crewed yacht — sleep anchored in quiet coves, swim off the back
Lesic Dimitri Palace in Korčula is a 6-suite boutique hotel in a medieval bishop's palace
Private yacht chef prepares every meal — fresh-caught fish, Croatian wines
Croatia's Adriatic coast has the clearest water in the Mediterranean

days.title: Arrival — Dubrovnik, the Pearl of the Adriatic

schedule.activity: Arrive Dubrovnik Airport, private transfer to Villa Dubrovnik (25 min along the coast road)
Check in to Villa Dubrovnik — perched on a cliff, the view of the Old Town walls hits you immediately
Take the villa's private elevator down to the sea-level terrace — swim in the crystal-clear Adriatic
Welcome dinner at Villa Dubrovnik's restaurant — terrace dining overlooking the lit-up Old Town walls, fresh Adriatic fish, local wine
Kids bedtime — the sound of gentle waves on the cliff
Adults nightcap on the terrace — Dubrovnik's old walls glowing gold
Breakfast at Villa Dubrovnik — eggs, prosciutto, Croatian olive oil, harbor views
Water taxi from the villa's pier to Old Town (5 min — arriving by sea is magical)
Walk the city walls — 2km circuit around the entire Old Town (baby in carrier, toddler in backpack carrier — no strollers on walls)
Explore the Stradun (main street) — ice cream at Dolce Vita, the 5-year-old runs through the marble streets
Lunch at Kopun — rooftop terrace, slow-roasted lamb, octopus salad, Old Town rooftop views
Nap time — water taxi back to villa, toddlers rest
Cable car to Srđ mountain — panoramic views of Dubrovnik, islands, the coast (the 5-year-old presses face to glass)
Swim at Villa Dubrovnik's pier — snorkeling, jumping off the platform, warm clear water
Dinner at Nautika — Dubrovnik's finest restaurant, at the Pile Gate entrance to Old Town, lobster, sea bass, sunset
Evening walk through the illuminated Old Town — the 5-year-old pretends to be a knight defending the walls
Breakfast at the villa
Ferry to Lokrum Island (10 min from Old Town) — the 'cursed island' with peacocks roaming everywhere, botanical gardens, and a Dead Sea-like salt lake
Explore Lokrum — kids chase peacocks (gently!), wade in the salt lake, explore the ruined monastery
Game of Thrones filming locations tour — the Iron Throne prop on Lokrum, the 5-year-old sits on it for photos
Lunch at Lokrum's café — simple but beautiful, under olive trees
Ferry back, nap time at villa
Private sea kayaking around the Old Town walls — seeing Dubrovnik from the water, through sea caves
Grandparents with toddlers at villa — pool, reading, Adriatic views
Sunset at Buža Bar — famous cliff-side bar carved into the city walls, drinks overlooking the sea (kids welcome before 7 PM)
Dinner at Proto — Old Town institution since 1886, seafood risotto, fresh oysters
Final breakfast at Villa Dubrovnik, check out
Board private crewed yacht at Gruž Harbor — meet your skipper and chef, safety briefing, settle into cabins
Sail to Lopud island (1 hr) — one of the Elafiti Islands, car-free paradise
Walk across Lopud to Šunj Beach — the best sandy beach near Dubrovnik, shallow warm water, perfect for toddlers
Lunch on the yacht — chef prepares fresh salad, grilled fish, Croatian olive oil, local wine
Nap time — yacht anchored in a quiet cove, gentle rocking, toddlers sleep like angels
Sail to Šipan island — swim off the yacht in a deserted cove, crystal water 20m visibility
Anchor at Šipan harbor — walk through the tiny village, visit ruins of old summer palaces, kids explore
Dinner at Kod Marka in Šipan — tiny family-run restaurant, whatever they caught today, cooked simply
Sleep on the yacht — anchored under the stars, gentle waves, absolute peace
Swim off the back of the yacht — morning dip in the Adriatic, coffee on deck
Breakfast on deck — chef's spread with local cheeses, figs, eggs
Sail to Mljet island (2 hrs) — ancient forests, two saltwater lakes inside the national park
Explore Mljet National Park — rent bikes or walk around the Great Lake, visit the island monastery, kayak the lake
Lunch at Konoba Ankora on Mljet — terrace over the lake, fresh mussels, grilled squid
Nap time on the yacht — anchor in Mljet's Pomena bay, kids sleep below deck
Sail toward Korčula (2.5 hrs) — dolphins often spotted in the Pelješac Channel!
Arrive Korčula — anchor off the medieval walled town, it glows golden at sunset
Dinghy ashore, walk the marble streets of Korčula Old Town — miniature Dubrovnik, less crowded
Dinner at LD Restaurant — Michelin-worthy, terrace on the sea wall, Dalmatian cuisine
Final night on the yacht — anchored beneath Korčula's walls
Final yacht breakfast on deck, then disembark and say farewell to crew
Check into Lesic Dimitri Palace — a 5-suite boutique hotel in a medieval bishop's palace
Explore Korčula Old Town — Marco Polo's alleged birthplace (climb the tower!), the cathedral, narrow fishbone-pattern streets
Moreška sword dance performance — traditional Korčulan knight battle dance, the 5-year-old is mesmerized
Lunch at Konoba Mate — hidden in an olive grove, homemade pasta, grilled fish, honest Dalmatian cooking
Nap time at the palace — thick stone walls keep it cool
Beach time at Pupnatska Luka — a hidden cove, clear water, pebble beach (bring water shoes for toddlers)
Wine tasting at Korčula's local winery — the island grows Grk and Pošip grapes found nowhere else
Dinner on the Lesic Dimitri terrace — sunset over the channel, fresh lobster, Korčulan wine
Breakfast at Lesic Dimitri, check out
Fast boat to Vis island area — visit the famous Blue Cave on Biševo island (swim inside a cave glowing electric blue)
Stop at Stiniva Beach — voted Europe's best beach, dramatic cove accessed through a narrow cliff opening
Lunch on Vis island at Konoba Roki's — farm-to-table in a vineyard, their own wine, bread, olive oil
Fast catamaran or private boat back to Dubrovnik (2.5 hrs, toddlers nap on board)
Check back into Villa Dubrovnik for final night — familiar comfort
Final swim at the villa's pier — soak in the Adriatic one last time
Farewell dinner at 360° — Dubrovnik's Michelin-starred restaurant, inside the Old Town walls, fortress views
Final walk through Dubrovnik's Old Town — gelato, photos, memories
Breakfast at Villa Dubrovnik — last Croatian coffee overlooking the Adriatic
Quick stop at Dubrovnik's morning market — buy olive oil, lavender, figs to take home
Transfer to Dubrovnik Airport
Fly to London Heathrow (2.5 hrs), connect to LAX (11 hrs)
Arrive LAX
Private car home — Mediterranean memories forever

tips: Late June is PERFECT for Croatia — warm, sunny, shoulder season means fewer crowds than July/August
The private yacht is the best way to see the islands with kids — nap anywhere, swim anytime, no schedules
Dubrovnik's Old Town has lots of stairs — strollers are difficult, bring a baby carrier
Water shoes are essential — most Croatian beaches are pebble, not sand
Croatian cuisine is underrated — the seafood rivals Italy's at half the price
The Blue Cave visits are weather-dependent — have a backup plan
Croatia uses the Euro as of 2023 — no currency exchange needed
Book Villa Dubrovnik's sea-view suites MONTHS in advance — it's small and exclusive

name: New Zealand (South Island — Queenstown + Kaikoura)
tagline: Whale spouts at dawn, glowworm galaxies underground, and hobbit holes in the greenest hills on Earth
weather: 38–55°F, NZ winter, crisp and clear, possible snow in mountains, stunning light

return.arrive: Jul 4, 11:00 PM LAX (cross date line back)

hotel.name: Matakauri Lodge (Queenstown, 3 nights) + Hapuku Lodge (Kaikoura, 2 nights) + The George (Christchurch, 1 night)

rooms.name: Matakauri — Lake Suite
rooms.for: Grandparents — floor-to-ceiling lake and mountain views, fireplace

rooms.name: Matakauri — Owner's Cottage (3BR)
rooms.for: Parents + kids — private house on the property, full kitchen, lake views

rooms.name: Hapuku Lodge — Treethouse Suite
rooms.for: Grandparents — perched in the canopy of a manuka grove

rooms.name: The George — Penthouse Suite
rooms.for: All — large enough for the family, park views

highlights: Matakauri overlooks Lake Wakatipu and the Remarkables — one of NZ's most spectacular settings
Owner's Cottage is essentially a private luxury home — ideal for families with kids
Hapuku's treehouses are 30ft above ground in a manuka canopy — magical for kids
Kaikoura has year-round sperm whale watching and dolphin swimming
New Zealand is the safest, most family-friendly country in the world

days.title: Travel Day — Cross the Date Line

schedule.activity: Depart LAX on Air New Zealand Business Premier — lie-flat beds, outstanding kids' program, NZ wine
13 hrs overnight — sleep across the Pacific, cross the International Date Line (skip June 28 entirely!)
Arrive Queenstown Airport — dramatic landing between mountains and lake
Private transfer to Matakauri Lodge (15 min) — jaw drops at the Remarkables mountain view
Check into Owner's Cottage — explore the 3BR private home, kids find the lake-view hot tub
Late breakfast at the lodge — NZ eggs, bacon, fresh sourdough, flat whites
Walk along the Queenstown lakefront — Queenstown Gardens, frisbee golf course, ducks on the lake
Lunch at Fergburger — Queenstown's legendary burger joint, massive portions, the 5-year-old gets the 'Little Lamby'
Nap time at the cottage — jet lag recovery, fireplace lit, mountain views
Skyline Gondola ride + luge — ride the gondola up Bob's Peak, then luge down (the 5-year-old rides with a parent, grandparents take the scenic route)
Hot tub at the cottage with mountain sunset — the Remarkables turn pink and gold
Dinner at Matakauri Lodge — five-course tasting menu with NZ wine, kids eat earlier in the cottage
Stargazing from the cottage deck — southern hemisphere constellations, Southern Cross

days.title: Milford Sound — Eighth Wonder of the World

schedule.activity: Early breakfast at the cottage — big day ahead
Scenic drive to Milford Sound (3.5 hrs via Te Anau — one of NZ's most beautiful drives, mirror lakes, Homer Tunnel)
Board Milford Sound cruise — 2-hour cruise through the fjord: Mitre Peak towering above, waterfalls cascading, seals on rocks, dolphins possible
Encounter the 'spray zone' under Stirling Falls — the boat sails close enough to get splashed, kids shriek with delight
Lunch on board — café-style, sandwiches, soup, hot drinks (it's winter and cold out!)
Drive back to Queenstown (3.5 hrs, all kids nap, stop at Te Anau for coffee and pie)
Arrive back at Matakauri — hot tub immediately to warm up
Dinner in Queenstown at Rata — Chef Josh Emett's modern NZ cuisine, lamb, venison, seafood
Back to cottage, cozy evening by the fire
Sleep in, breakfast at Matakauri
Visit Walter Peak High Country Farm — take the vintage TSS Earnslaw steamship across Lake Wakatipu (the 5-year-old watches the coal-fired engines)
Sheep shearing demonstration, farm tour — kids bottle-feed lambs, meet farm dogs, Scottish Highlander cattle
Colonel's Homestead morning tea — scones, cream, jam, overlooking the lake
Steamship back to Queenstown
Nap time at the cottage
Drive to Arrowtown (20 min) — adorable gold-rush era village, kids pan for gold in the Arrow River
Explore Arrowtown's main street — Remarkable Sweet Shop (candy heaven for kids), craft stores, autumn-gold trees (winter light is beautiful)
Hot chocolate at Provisions café — the best in the region
Dinner at Aosta — Italian-NZ fusion in Arrowtown, wood-fired pizza, fresh pasta
Back to Matakauri for final Queenstown night
Breakfast and check out of Matakauri
Fly Queenstown to Christchurch (1 hr) — beautiful approach over the Canterbury Plains
Pick up rental SUV at Christchurch Airport, drive north to Kaikoura (2.5 hrs along spectacular coastal road)
Arrive Kaikoura — lunch at The Pier Hotel overlooking the ocean, famous crayfish (Kaikoura literally means 'eat crayfish' in Maori)
Check into Hapuku Lodge — nap time, the treehouse suites are 30ft up in the canopy, kids think it's a real treehouse
Ohau Stream Walk — short, easy walk to a waterfall where NZ fur seal pups play in the pool (incredibly cute, the 5-year-old is silent with wonder)
Kaikoura Peninsula Walkway — coastal path past seal colonies, seabirds, snow-capped Kaikoura Ranges behind
Dinner at Hapuku Lodge — their own olive oil, venison, Canterbury lamb, organic garden
Southern sky stargazing from the treehouse deck
Early breakfast at Hapuku — the whale boats leave early
Whale Watch Kaikoura — boat trip to see giant sperm whales (year-round residents, 95% success rate), also dusky dolphins, albatross, Hector's dolphins
Return to shore — buy paua (abalone) shell souvenirs at the Whale Watch gift shop
Nin's Bin — famous roadside caravan selling the freshest crayfish in NZ, eaten at a picnic table with mountain views
Nap time at the lodge
Dolphin Encounter Kaikoura — swim with wild dusky dolphins (for adults/5-year-old with wetsuit) or watch from the boat (grandparents + toddlers)
Kaikoura Lavender Farm — wander the purple fields with mountain backdrop, buy lavender honey
Dinner at Zephyr Restaurant in Kaikoura — crayfish three ways, Pacific Rim cuisine, local wines
Final treehouse evening — listen to the morepork owls calling
Breakfast at Hapuku, check out
Drive south to Christchurch (2.5 hrs, scenic coast road, toddlers nap)
Arrive Christchurch, check into The George — elegant boutique hotel overlooking Hagley Park
Lunch at The George's Pescatore restaurant — NZ seafood, garden views
Nap time at hotel
Christchurch Botanic Gardens — 21 hectares, kids run on the lawns, feed ducks, the conservatory is warm and tropical
Ride the Christchurch Heritage Tram through the rebuilt city center — see how the city recovered from the earthquake
International Antarctic Centre — penguin encounter, snowstorm simulator, Hagglund ride (all-terrain vehicle) — the 5-year-old goes wild
Farewell dinner at Inati — Christchurch's best restaurant, shared-plate NZ dining concept
Pack for departure

days.title: Fly Home — Cross the Date Line Back

schedule.activity: Early wake-up, grab-and-go breakfast from hotel
Fly Christchurch to Auckland (1.5 hrs)
Connect to Air New Zealand Business Premier to LAX (12 hrs)
Arrive LAX — you arrive BEFORE you left thanks to the date line!
Home recovery — July 5 is a rest day, recount tales of whales, glowworms, and hobbits

tips: June/July is WINTER in NZ — pack warm layers, but it's the quietest tourist season and scenery is stunning with snow on peaks
Kaikoura whale watching has 95%+ success rate — sperm whales are resident year-round in the deep canyon offshore
Air New Zealand Business Premier is one of the world's best — herringbone lie-flat, NZ wine, outstanding service
Car seats: NZ law requires them, arrange with rental company
Winter in Queenstown means no crowds at Milford Sound — you may have the fjord nearly to yourselves
Fergburger in Queenstown ALWAYS has a line — go at 11 AM to beat it
The Ohau Stream seal pup walk is free and one of NZ's best wildlife experiences — keep 10m distance from seals
NZ is arguably the safest country in the world for families — everyone is incredibly welcoming to kids

name: Italian Lakes — Como & Garda

weather: 68–85°F, warm and sunny with occasional thunderstorms

Lefay Resort: hilltop spa overlooking Lake Garda, infinity pools, organic gardens

Walking distance to Bellagio ferry from Tremezzo

Lunch at Bilacus — traditional trattoria, risotto, grilled fish
Nap time — ferry back to Tremezzo, nap at hotel
Hotel pool afternoon — floating pool on the lake is magical
Dinner at La Darsena in Tremezzo village — casual lakeside pizza, perfect for kids

Lunch at Locanda dell'Isola Comacina — tiny island restaurant, fire ritual farewell tradition
Nap time at hotel

Aperitivo at hotel terrace — Aperol spritz for adults, fresh juice for kids

Breakfast and check out of Grand Hotel Tremezzo

Lunch inside Gardaland — family restaurant in the Peppa Pig area
Nap break on benches near the gentle Carousel — 2.5 yr old and baby rest while 5 yr old does more rides

Drive to Lefay Resort & Spa (30 min)
Check in at Lefay Resort — hilltop with panoramic lake views

Breakfast at Lefay — overlooking the lake

Scaligero Castle — drawbridge over the moat (kids pretend to be knights), climb the tower (grandparents stay in the courtyard)

Lunch in Sirmione old town — fresh-caught lake perch, homemade pasta
Nap time — drive back to Lefay, rest at hotel

Gelato walk in Gargnano village (10 min from hotel) — charming fishing village, less touristy
Dinner at Lefay's Trattoria la Vigna — rustic Italian feast
Breakfast at Lefay
Ferry to Limone sul Garda (30 min) — the lemon town! Terraced lemon groves, pastel houses
Visit a limonaia (lemon greenhouse) — taste fresh lemon juice, lemon cake, limoncello for adults
Bike path along the lake (parents + 5 yr old rent bikes, grandparents walk the promenade with littles)
Lunch at lakeside trattoria — lake fish, bruschetta, fresh salad
Nap time — ferry back to Gargnano, rest at hotel
Olive oil farm visit near hotel — taste fresh oil, learn about pressing, walk through the grove
Final swim in Lefay's infinity pool — sunset over Lake Garda
Farewell dinner at Villa Feltrinelli (splurge, next door) — one of Italy's finest restaurants
Final breakfast at Lefay

Quick stop in Desenzano del Garda — lakefront gelato, souvenir shopping, playground
Lunch in Desenzano — casual pizza al taglio (by the slice)
Nap in the car — drive partway to Milan area for overnight (1 hr)
Check into airport area hotel (Sheraton Milan Malpensa)
Pool time at hotel — easy evening

Early bedtime — flight tomorrow

Shuttle to Milan Malpensa Airport (10 min from hotel)
Flight MXP → LAX via FRA/ZRH
Arrive at LAX

Italian restaurants typically don't open for dinner until 7:30 PM — ask for early seating at hotel restaurants
Bring water shoes — many lake beaches are pebbly, not sandy
Gardaland gets crowded on weekends — go on a weekday if your schedule allows
The hotel can arrange a private boat with captain for flexible lake exploration
Highchairs are standard in Italian restaurants — Italians adore children and will fuss over yours

name: Banwa Private Island, Palawan, Philippines

name: Ireland — Castle Country

Cinema, billiard room, archery, fishing on Lough Corrib
