Breakfast at castle
Visit a working sheep farm (30 min drive) — sheepdog demonstration, lamb bottle-feeding (2.5 yr old's dream!), shearing demonstration
Drive through Connemara — stone walls, peat bogs, wild ponies, mountain views. Stop at Kylemore Abbey (beautiful lakeside abbey and Victorian walled garden)
Lunch at Kylemore Abbey café — homemade scones, soup, sandwiches
Nap time in car — drive the scenic Sky Road (30 min loop)
Back at castle — horseback riding on the estate (5 yr old on lead rein, parents ride alongside)
Grandparents take a lake cruise on Lough Corrib — peaceful and scenic
Pub lunch culture — afternoon pint at local pub in Cong village, kids welcome, live trad music often playing
Dinner at George V Dining Room — final formal dinner
Leisurely breakfast — slow morning
Castle Cubs kids' club (ages 4+) — treasure hunt in the castle, sword-making craft, storytelling. 5 yr old joins
Tree-top zip line on estate (Zip-it at Ashford) — thrilling for grandparents who are game!
Toddler and baby lakeside walk with one parent — ducks, swans, gardens
Explore Cong village — Quiet Man museum (classic film was shot here), stone bridges, river walk
Pub lunch at Pat Cohan's Bar — the pub from The Quiet Man film, classic Irish stew
Nap time at castle
Fishing on Lough Corrib — guided catch-and-release, 5 yr old tries with a simple rod
Final afternoon tea — savor every scone
Farewell dinner at Wilde's with live trad music — tin whistle, fiddle, bodhrán
Breakfast at castle
Drive to Galway City (45 min)
Walk the colorful Latin Quarter — buskers, craft shops, Galway Market (Saturdays) with local cheeses, honey, crafts
Galway Atlantaquaria — Ireland's national aquarium, touch tanks, seals, ray feeding. Small enough to not overwhelm toddlers
Lunch at McDonagh's — Galway's legendary fish and chips since 1902
Nap time — drive back to castle, rest
Free afternoon at castle — croquet on the lawn, lakeside walk, gift shop
Packing for departure tomorrow
Early farewell dinner
Bedtime — early morning tomorrow
Final breakfast at Ashford Castle
Check out — fond farewell to castle staff
Drive toward Shannon (1.5 hrs), stop at Bunratty Castle & Folk Park — reconstructed Irish village, wax figures, farmhouses, school. 5 yr old can explore, very hands-on
Durty Nelly's pub next to Bunratty — one last pub lunch, kids welcome
Nap time — drive to Shannon area hotel (20 min), rest
Souvenir shopping in Shannon — Aran sweaters, Claddagh rings, leprechaun toys for kids
Early dinner near Shannon
Early bedtime at Shannon hotel
Breakfast and check out
Transfer to Shannon Airport (15 min)
Flight SNN → LAX
Arrive at LAX

tips: Rain gear is NON-NEGOTIABLE — pack waterproof jackets, pants, and stroller rain covers for everyone
Irish pubs are incredibly family-friendly until about 7 PM — don't hesitate to bring kids
Cliffs of Moher can be very windy — use baby carriers not strollers, hold toddler hands firmly
Ashford Castle has wellies (rain boots) to borrow in all sizes — even kids'
Book the falconry experience in advance — it fills up fast
Irish people will talk to your children constantly — it's genuine warmth, embrace it

name: Basque Coast, Spain
tagline: Pintxos crawls, world-class surf beaches, and the Guggenheim with the whole family
weather: 62–78°F, warm and pleasant, occasional Basque drizzle, perfect beach weather

outbound.route: LAX → MAD/CDG → EAS (San Sebastián) or BIO (Bilbao)

hotel.name: Hotel Maria Cristina, a Luxury Collection Hotel
highlights: Belle Époque palace on the Urumea River, steps from La Concha beach
Where movie stars stay during San Sebastián Film Festival
Rooftop terrace with city views
Walking distance to Old Town pintxos bars
Concierge arranges private food tours and cooking classes

schedule.activity: Arrive at San Sebastián Airport (or Bilbao + 1 hr transfer) — private car to Hotel Maria Cristina (15 min)
Check in — the grand lobby alone is worth the trip
Lunch at La Cuchara de San Telmo (Old Town) — legendary pintxos bar, foie gras toast, grilled mushrooms, jamón
Nap time — jet lag and food coma
First walk on La Concha beach — the crescent-shaped city beach, gentle waves, fine sand. Kids wade in the shallow water. Possibly the most beautiful urban beach in Europe
Walk the Paseo de la Concha promenade — elegant railings, ocean views, ice cream from a kiosk
Dinner at Bodega Donostiarra — classic Basque restaurant, grilled txuletón (giant rib steak to share), simple kids' options
Breakfast at hotel — churros con chocolate, fresh fruit, Spanish tortilla
Aquarium of San Sebastián — oceanarium tunnel walk-through, touch pool, shark tanks. Small enough to not overwhelm toddlers (1 hr visit)
Walk through the Old Town (Parte Vieja) — narrow streets, pintxos bars with counters loaded with food on toothpicks. Kids grab croquetas and tortilla slices
Guided pintxos crawl (3 bars, private guide) — the guide picks the best bites at each stop. San Sebastián has more Michelin stars per capita than anywhere in the world
Nap time at hotel
La Concha beach afternoon — rent a beach umbrella, build sandcastles, wade in the bay. Protected bay means gentle waves, perfect for toddlers
Walk up Monte Urgull (easy paved path) — old fortress at top, panoramic views of the bay and city. Short enough for grandparents
Dinner at Gandarias — famous Old Town pintxos bar with sit-down area, solomillo (sirloin) pintxo is legendary
Breakfast at hotel
Drive to Zumaia (40 min) — the Flysch coastal cliffs

Flysch boat tour (1.5 hrs) — dramatic geological cliffs that tell 60 million years of Earth's history in rock layers. Filmed for Game of Thrones (Dragonstone!). The 5 yr old thinks it's a dragon island
Lunch in Zumaia — grilled fresh fish at Bedua restaurant, ocean views
Nap time in the car — drive back to San Sebastián
Zurriola Beach — the surfer beach. Watch surfers from the promenade, or wade in the gentler areas. Different vibe from La Concha
Kursaal building — striking modern architecture by the beach, sometimes has family exhibitions
Dinner at Txepetxa — the anchovy pintxos bar (sounds weird, tastes incredible). Plus croquetas and cheese for kids
Breakfast at hotel
Private car to Bilbao (1 hr 15 min) — scenic Basque motorway through green hills

Guggenheim Museum Bilbao — Frank Gehry's titanium masterpiece. Giant Puppy (flower-covered dog sculpture) out front is a kid magnet. Inside: family-friendly exhibitions, kids' audio guide. The building itself amazes everyone
Maman (giant spider sculpture) and outdoor art — Tulips by Jeff Koons, Fog sculpture. 5 yr old runs around the outdoor installations
Lunch at Nerua (Guggenheim's Michelin-star restaurant) — worth the splurge, kids' menu available. OR casual Café Guggenheim
Nap time — stroll along the Nervión River or nap in the car
Bilbao Old Town (Casco Viejo) — Seven Streets, pintxos, Cathedral, Plaza Nueva
Pintxos at Plaza Nueva — multiple bars around the square, each with specialties
Drive back to San Sebastián (1 hr 15 min)
Light dinner — pintxos at hotel or nearby
Breakfast at hotel
Basque cooking class (private, 2.5 hrs) — learn to make pintxos, Basque cheesecake, and tortilla. Kids help crack eggs, roll dough. Toddler has own little station with play-dough to pretend-cook
Eat your creations for lunch!
Nap time at hotel
Isla de Santa Clara — take the small ferry to the island in the middle of La Concha bay (15 min). Tiny beach, lighthouse, peaceful. Like your own private island. Calm water for wading
Ferry back — golden hour over the bay
Funicular up Monte Igueldo — old-fashioned amusement park at the top with rickety rides and THE most spectacular view of the bay. 5 yr old loves the vintage rides
Dinner at Arzak or Martín Berasategui (adults alternate, one pair babysits) — two of Spain's greatest restaurants. OR family dinner at Narru
Breakfast at hotel
Drive to Getaria (25 min) — charming fishing village, birthplace of Balenciaga and Elcano (first to circumnavigate the globe)
Walk through Getaria — mouse-shaped island (Ratón de Getaria), fishing boats in harbor, narrow medieval streets
Txakoli wine tasting at a vineyard overlooking the ocean — Basque sparkling white wine, poured from height. Grape juice for kids, vineyard walk
Lunch at Elkano — the world-famous grilled turbot restaurant. Whole fish grilled over charcoal. Life-changing. Kids get simple grilled fish
Nap time — drive to Zarautz beach (5 min)
Zarautz beach — longest beach in the Basque Country, wide sand, gentle waves
Drive back to San Sebastián
Dinner at Bar Nestor — famous for only making 2 tortillas per day (arrive at 1 PM to reserve a slice for dinner!). Also grilled txuletón
Breakfast at hotel
Drive to Hondarribia (20 min) — colorful fortified town on the French border, one of the prettiest in the Basque Country
Walk through the medieval old town — Charles V castle, painted wooden balconies, Calle San Pedro fishing district
Quick ferry across the river to Hendaye, France (5 min) — kids love the border crossing. French beach is huge and sandy
Hendaye beach — enormous flat sandy beach, very shallow water extending far out. Perfect for toddlers
Lunch on Hendaye beach — casual crêperie
Nap time on the beach (umbrella shade) or drive back to hotel
Back in San Sebastián — last Old Town pintxos crawl, all the favorites one more time
Packing at hotel
Farewell dinner at Kokotxa — Old Town, creative Basque cuisine, Michelin-starred
Final breakfast at hotel
Last walk on La Concha beach — barefoot in the sand, one more time
San Telmo Museum — Basque culture museum in a converted convent, kid-friendly interactive sections
Final pintxos lunch in the Old Town — bar-hop your greatest hits list
Nap time and final packing
Paseo Nuevo coastal walk — dramatic waves crashing on the sea wall, kids get sprayed!
Early farewell dinner — final Basque cheesecake
Early bedtime — early flight
Early breakfast (packed by hotel)
Transfer to San Sebastián or Bilbao Airport
Flight EAS/BIO → MAD/CDG → LAX
Arrive at LAX

tips: Spanish dinner time is 9-10 PM — pintxos bars open earlier (7 PM) which works better with kids
La Concha beach has a tidal pool area (protected shallow water) — perfect for toddlers at low tide
Book Arzak or Martín Berasategui weeks in advance — these are bucket-list restaurants
Pintxos are typically 2-3 euros each — it's the best value fine dining in the world
The Basque language (Euskara) is on all signs — it's unrelated to any other language, fascinating
Siesta is real here — many shops close 2-5 PM, which conveniently aligns with nap time

name: Lofoten Islands, Norway
tagline: Midnight sun, Viking legends, and eagles soaring over arctic fishing villages
weather: 50–65°F, long daylight (near 24hrs), mild Arctic summer, occasional rain

hotel.name: Nusfjord Arctic Resort + Hattvika Lodge
highlights: Historic restored fisherman cabins on the harbor
Private hot tubs overlooking the fjord
On-site restaurant with Arctic seafood
Walking paths suitable for strollers along the waterfront
Midnight sun visible 24 hours in late June

schedule.activity: Arrive at Leknes Airport, private transfer to Nusfjord Arctic Resort (45 min scenic drive)
Check into rorbu cabins, unpack and explore the historic harbor village
Stroller-friendly walk along the wooden boardwalks of Nusfjord village
Welcome dinner at Karoline Restaurant — fresh-caught cod, king crab, local lamb
Hot tub session at the cabin watching the midnight sun over the fjord
Bedtime for kids (blackout curtains essential — it never gets dark!)
Adults enjoy wine on the deck in full golden-hour sunlight
Breakfast in the cabin — scrambled eggs, smoked salmon, fresh bread from the village bakery
Lofotr Viking Museum — reconstructed longhouse, Viking ship, axe throwing, dress-up costumes for kids
Lunch at the Viking Feast Hall — traditional Viking-style meal cooked over open fire
Nap time — drive back to cabins, toddlers nap, 5-year-old quiet time with Viking coloring books
Explore Reine village — the iconic red cabins on stilts, gentle waterfront stroll
Visit the Lofoten Stockfish Museum — learn how Vikings preserved fish for trade
Dinner at Anitas Sjømat in Sakrisøy — famous fish burgers and fresh seafood
Evening beach play at Haukland Beach — white sand, turquoise arctic water (too cold to swim but gorgeous)
Kids bedtime, adults midnight sun photography walk
Breakfast at cabin — waffles, brown cheese, berries
Private RIB boat safari into Trollfjord — guaranteed white-tailed sea eagle sightings, dramatic narrow fjord walls
Stop at Digermulen for hot cocoa and cinnamon rolls at a harbor café
Lunch at Børsen Spiseri in Svolvær — harbor-view restaurant, kids menu available
Nap time back at cabins — drive from Svolvær (1 hr scenic route)
Lofoten Aquarium (Lofotakvariet) in Kabelvåg — seals, otters, Arctic fish species, touch pools for kids
Free play at the harbor — kids watch fishing boats come in with the day's catch
Dinner at cabin — grandparents cook fresh cod bought from fishermen at the harbor
Midnight sun kayaking for adults (calm harbor, guided, while grandparents watch kids)
Early breakfast — the whale boats leave early from Andøya (2 hr drive north)
Scenic drive north to Andenes via the coastal road, stopping at viewpoints
Whale watching safari with Whalesafari Andenes — sperm whales, orcas possible, puffins in the water
Lunch in Andenes at Lysthuset Restaurant — fish soup and open-faced sandwiches
Nap time — toddlers sleep in car on the drive back south
Stop at Uttakleiv Beach — heart-shaped rock formations, tidal pools, kids explore
Visit a local glass-blowing workshop in Vikten — watch artisans at work, small gallery
Dinner at Underhuset Restaurant in Reine — lamb shank, fresh halibut, kids portions
Evening walk to Reinebringen viewpoint base (gentle section only) for photos
Lazy breakfast at the cabin — pancakes, cloudberries, yogurt
Puffin boat trip from Røst island — thousands of nesting Atlantic puffins on sea cliffs
Picnic lunch packed by the resort — eat at a scenic overlook
Nap time at cabins — quiet afternoon, coloring and reading
Visit a local goat farm — kids pet goats, see traditional Norwegian farm life
Family fishing from the harbor dock — simple handlines, guaranteed catches of cod and pollock
The resort chef cooks your catch for dinner at Karoline Restaurant — a 'hook to plate' experience
Bonfire on the rocky beach with marshmallows — midnight sun glowing on the water
Kids to bed, adults sauna session with fjord views
Breakfast at cabin
Drive to Henningsvær — the 'Venice of Lofoten,' colorful houses on tiny islands connected by bridges
Visit Kaviar Factory contemporary art gallery — world-class art in a converted fish factory
Stroll through Henningsvær — ice cream at Lysstøperiet café, watch kids play by the harbor
Lunch at Fiskekrogen — one of Norway's best seafood restaurants, right on the harbor
Nap time — scenic drive back, kids sleep in car
Private guided sea kayak in calm harbor waters — tandem kayaks, grandparent-friendly pace
Beach time at Ramberg Beach — white sand, shallow wading for the 5-year-old
Farewell Lofoten dinner at the cabin — BBQ on the terrace with local ingredients
Breakfast and pack up cabins
Flight from Leknes to Oslo (1.5 hrs)
Arrive Oslo, check into The Thief hotel on Tjuvholmen waterfront
Nap time at hotel — toddlers rest after travel
Astrup Fearnley Museum sculpture park — outdoor art kids can climb and touch, waterfront promenade
Oslo Reptile Park — snakes, lizards, turtles, small but the 5-year-old will love it
Dinner at Aker Brygge waterfront — Lofoten Fiskerestaurant (yes, named after where you just were!)
Walk along the Oslo Opera House roof — sloped design kids can run up, harbor views
Kids bedtime at hotel
Breakfast at The Thief — excellent buffet with harbor views
Vigeland Sculpture Park — 200+ sculptures, huge open lawns for kids to run, iconic Monolith
The new Museum of the Viking Age (opened 2025) — immersive Viking ship exhibits, kids' interactive zone
Lunch at Bygdøy Beach café — casual, kids play on the sandy beach while you eat
Nap time at hotel
Norwegian Museum of Science and Technology — hands-on exhibits, water play area for kids
Free time at hotel — pool time, snacks
Final dinner at Maaemo (3 Michelin stars) — advance reservation essential, tasting menu celebrating Norwegian nature
Pack and prepare for departure
Breakfast at hotel
Private transfer to Oslo Gardermoen Airport (OSL)
Check in at SAS Business Class lounge — kids play area available
Depart Oslo for LAX (11h direct or via connection)
Arrive LAX (same day, time travel magic of westbound flights)
Private car home — trip complete!

tips: Pack blackout sleep masks and portable blackout curtains — the midnight sun means it NEVER gets dark in late June
Bring warm layers even in summer — temps can drop to 45°F on boat trips
Car seats are required by Norwegian law; pre-arrange with rental company or bring your own
Motion sickness meds for whale watching — the Andøya trip goes into open ocean
Book the whale safari well in advance — summer is peak season
Mosquitoes can be fierce in sheltered areas; bring DEET-free kid-safe repellent
The Lofoten Aquarium is small but excellent for toddlers — plan 1–1.5 hours

name: Oman (Muscat + Jebel Akhdar)
tagline: Turtle hatchlings, desert stargazing, and swimming in emerald wadis under ancient frankincense skies
weather: 85–105°F coast, 70–85°F mountains, dry, very hot at sea level

hotel.name: The Chedi Muscat (4 nights) + Alila Jabal Akhdar (3 nights) + Desert Nights Camp (1 night)

rooms.name: Alila Jabal Akhdar — Mountain View Suite
Desert Nights Camp — Royal Suite Tent

highlights: The Chedi has the longest pool in Oman (103m), set amid manicured gardens
Alila sits on the edge of a 2,000m canyon — jaw-dropping views
Desert Nights Camp offers luxury Bedouin tents with AC and private bathrooms
Omani people are famously hospitable and adore children
Both luxury hotels offer babysitting and kids' activity programs

schedule.activity: Arrive Muscat International Airport, private transfer to The Chedi Muscat (20 min)
Express check-in with Omani dates and kahwa coffee welcome, freshen up
Breakfast at The Restaurant — eggs to order, Arabic mezze, tropical fruits
Pool time at the 103m Long Pool — shallow kids area, cabanas with misters for grandparents
Nap time — rooms are cool, dark, perfect for jet-lag recovery
Gentle walk through the Chedi gardens — koi ponds, fountains, the 5-year-old spots lizards
Sunset at Qurum Beach — wade in warm Arabian Sea water, build sand castles
Dinner at The Beach Restaurant — grilled hammour fish, lamb ouzi, ocean breeze
Early bedtime — adjust to Oman time
Early morning dolphin watching boat trip from Marina Bandar Al Rowdha — hundreds of spinner dolphins in Muscat harbor
Breakfast back at The Chedi after the trip
Visit Muttrah Souq — ancient covered market, frankincense, spices, tiny shops, the 5-year-old picks a treasure
Photo stop at Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque — the enormous crystal chandelier amazes kids, must dress modestly
Lunch at Bait Al Luban in Muttrah — Omani shuwa (slow-cooked lamb), harbor views
Nap time at hotel — pool for adults while toddlers sleep
Oman Aquarium at Mall of Muscat — sharks, rays, coral reef tunnel walk-through (shark-obsessed 5-year-old heaven)
Visit the Royal Opera House gardens — beautiful architecture, gelato from the café
Dinner at Kargeen Café — enchanting garden restaurant with cushioned seating, Omani-Indian fusion
Breakfast at The Chedi, then pack day bags
Private SUV drive to Ras Al Jinz Turtle Reserve (2.5 hrs via coastal road, scenic stops)
Stop at Bimmah Sinkhole — stunning turquoise natural pool, kids can wade in shallow areas
Lunch at a seaside restaurant in Sur — grilled kingfish, rice
Nap time — drive to Ras Al Jinz, toddlers sleep in car seats
Check into Ras Al Jinz Turtle Centre, settle in, visit the small museum about green sea turtles
Beach walk — look for turtle tracks in the sand, hermit crabs, the 5-year-old becomes a junior ranger
Dinner at the centre's restaurant — simple but good, early meal before the main event
Guided turtle nesting watch — green sea turtles haul up the beach to lay eggs under the stars (peak season!)

days.title: Wadi Shab & Drive to the Mountains

schedule.activity: Optional: early morning turtle hatchling release (seasonal, ask rangers) — baby turtles to the sea!
Breakfast at the turtle centre
Drive to Wadi Shab (1.5 hrs) — one of Oman's most beautiful canyon swimming holes
Boat crossing and gentle hike into Wadi Shab — emerald pools, the 5-year-old splashes in warm rock pools
schedule.who: All (baby in carrier, grandparents do gentle section)

schedule.activity: Picnic lunch by the wadi pools — pre-packed by hotel
Drive to Jebel Akhdar / Alila resort (3 hrs, toddlers nap in car, dramatic mountain switchbacks)
Arrive Alila Jabal Akhdar — check in, jaws drop at the canyon views from 2,000m elevation
Cool mountain air — explore the infinity pool perched on the cliff edge
Dinner at Juniper Restaurant — locally foraged herbs, mountain lamb, pomegranate dishes
Stargazing from the terrace — zero light pollution, Milky Way visible, hotel provides telescopes
Breakfast at Alila — mountain views, fresh rose jam on toast (Jebel Akhdar is famous for roses)
Guided walk through Al Ayn rose village — ancient terraced gardens, pomegranate and walnut orchards
Visit a traditional rosewater distillery — watch the distillation process, buy rosewater for grandma
Lunch at Alila — poolside dining, kids menu
Nap time — suites have amazing mountain silence, deep sleep
Via Ferrata adventure on the cliff face (adults only, guided, Alila's signature experience)
Grandparents take kids to the Alila spa garden — gentle sensory walk, herb picking
Afternoon tea on the terrace — Omani halwa, dates, cardamom tea
Dinner at the resort — chef's table experience with mountain-to-table menu
Final mountain breakfast at Alila
Check out and drive to Wahiba Sands desert (3 hrs, kids nap en route)
Arrive Desert Nights Camp — check into luxury Bedouin-style tents with AC
Lunch at the camp — Arabic buffet in the main tent
Nap time — surprisingly cool inside the tents
Gentle camel ride through the dunes — the 5-year-old rides with a parent, toddlers in a carrier on a calm camel
Dune bashing in a 4x4 SUV — thrilling but optional, grandparents can watch sunset from camp instead
Sunset from the top of a massive dune — the kids sand-surf down
BBQ dinner under the stars — traditional Omani shuwa pit-roast lamb, Arabic coffee, dates
Stargazing in the desert — no light pollution, shooting stars, Bedouin stories from the guide

days.title: Return to Muscat — Final Beach Day

schedule.activity: Sunrise over the dunes — magical orange light, photo opportunity
Breakfast at camp
Drive back to Muscat (3 hrs, scenic route through Hajar Mountains)
Arrive back at The Chedi Muscat, check in to rooms
Lunch and nap time — The Chedi pool, rest and decompress
The Chedi Spa — Omani frankincense massage for grandparents and adults (hotel babysitting for kids)
Kids pool time with hotel childcare team — splash pool, floaties
Last Muscat sunset on the beach — warm Arabian Sea, final sandcastle
Farewell dinner at The Chedi — The Arabian Courtyard, lantern-lit, Omani feast
Breakfast at The Chedi — last chance for shakshuka and Arabic breads
Last pool session or walk through the gardens
Check out, pick up last-minute frankincense and Omani souvenirs from hotel shop
Private transfer to Muscat Airport
Depart Muscat via Doha on Qatar Airways (next day early AM redeye)
Arrive LAX — welcome home!
Private car home

tips: June/July is HOT in Oman — schedule all outdoor Muscat activities before 10 AM or after 5 PM
Jebel Akhdar is 15–20°F cooler than Muscat, a welcome relief at 2,000m elevation
Turtle nesting season peaks June–August — you're almost guaranteed to see nesting green turtles
Dress modestly when visiting mosques — cover shoulders and knees, women cover hair
Oman is one of the safest countries in the Middle East — extremely low crime, friendly people
Bring reef-safe sunscreen — the wadi ecosystems are fragile
Qatar Airways Qsuites are essentially private rooms — great for families with kids

name: Sri Lanka
tagline: Wild elephants at sunrise, ancient temples in the clouds, and whale sharks off the southern coast
weather: 78–88°F, dry season on south/west coast, tropical, occasional rain

hotel.name: Cape Weligama (South Coast, 4 nights) + Wild Coast Tented Lodge (Yala, 2 nights) + Amangalla (Galle, 1 night)

rooms.name: Cape Weligama — Moon Pool Villa
rooms.for: Grandparents — private pool, clifftop, ocean view

rooms.name: Cape Weligama — Sun Pool Villa (2BR)
rooms.for: Parents + 3 kids — expansive villa, garden pool, family-configured

rooms.name: Wild Coast — Cocoon Suite
rooms.for: Grandparents — glamping pod with AC, outdoor shower

highlights: Cape Weligama is a Resplendent Ceylon property — Sri Lanka's finest luxury brand
Wild Coast Tented Lodge sits at the entrance to Yala National Park — leopards sometimes walk past the 'cocoons'
Amangalla is an Aman resort inside the 17th-century Galle Fort walls
Sri Lanka has the highest density of leopards in the world (Yala National Park)
Blue whales migrate through southern Sri Lankan waters year-round

schedule.activity: Arrive Colombo (CMB), private transfer waiting — drive to Cape Weligama (3 hrs south, kids sleep in car)
Arrive Cape Weligama — late check-in, snacks left in villa, crash into beds
Breakfast at the cape — hoppers (Sri Lankan bowl-shaped pancakes), egg hoppers, tropical fruit, ocean infinity view
Pool and beach morning — Cape Weligama has a crescent beach below the cliff, warm Indian Ocean, gentle waves
Watch the stilt fishermen at Koggala — iconic Sri Lankan image, fishermen perched on poles in the surf
Lunch at the resort — Sri Lankan rice and curry, fresh fish, king coconut water
Nap time — villa pool for parents while toddlers sleep
Visit the Sea Turtle Hatchery at Koggala — hold baby sea turtles, learn about conservation (the 5-year-old is gentle and amazed)
Tuk-tuk ride through Weligama village — kids wave to everyone, stop for fresh mango and curd at a roadside stall
Sunset from the Cape Weligama infinity pool — possibly the best pool sunset in Sri Lanka
Dinner at the resort's Terrace Restaurant — seafood BBQ, hoppers, Sri Lankan curries
Early departure for Mirissa Harbor (15 min) — whale watching boat trip, blue whales and sperm whales frequent these waters year-round
Whale watching safari — scan the horizon for whale spouts, spinner dolphins playing in the bow wave
Return to shore, breakfast back at the resort — big appetite after the morning ocean
Drive to Galle Fort (30 min) — 17th-century Dutch colonial fortress, UNESCO World Heritage Site
Walk the Galle Fort ramparts — ocean views, lighthouse, the 5-year-old pretends to be a pirate defending the fort
Lunch at Fort Bazaar — boutique hotel restaurant inside the fort, Sri Lankan-fusion cuisine
Nap time — drive back to Cape Weligama, toddlers sleep
Sri Lankan cooking class at the resort — learn to make hoppers, dhal curry, pol sambol with the chef
Villa pool time — warm evening, tropical birds calling
Dinner at Lucky Fort Restaurant in Galle — casual, incredible crab curry, ocean view
Early breakfast at the villa
Drive to Udawalawe National Park (2 hrs) — Sri Lanka's best park for wild elephants
Jeep safari in Udawalawe — herds of wild Asian elephants (sometimes 100+ visible), water buffalo, crocodiles, peacocks, eagles
Elephant Transit Home (near the park) — orphaned baby elephants being rehabilitated for release, bottle-feeding time
Lunch at a local hotel near the park — rice and curry, kottu roti
Drive back to Cape Weligama (2 hrs, kids nap the whole way)
Pool and rest time at the villa — decompress after a big safari day
Ayurvedic spa treatment for grandparents at Cape Weligama — traditional Sri Lankan healing massage
Sunset cocktails on the cape
Dinner at the resort — final night at Cape Weligama, fresh lobster, arrack cocktails
Breakfast and check out of Cape Weligama
Scenic drive east to Yala / Wild Coast Tented Lodge (3 hrs along the southern coast)
Arrive Wild Coast Tented Lodge — check into the extraordinary cocoon-shaped glamping suites
Lunch at the lodge — open-air dining, the cocoons visible through the trees
Nap time — the tented suites have AC and are surprisingly luxurious, jungle sounds surround you
Afternoon Yala National Park safari — search for Sri Lankan leopards (highest density in the world), elephants, sloth bears, crocodiles, hundreds of bird species
Sundowner drinks in the park — the guide finds a scenic spot for sunset with cold drinks
Dinner at the lodge — campfire dining, storytelling, the sounds of the jungle at night
Night sounds walk (within the lodge grounds, guided) — spot jungle cats, civets, owls
Dawn safari in Yala — the best time for leopard sightings, mist over the lagoons, elephants at water holes
Return to lodge for breakfast — recounting what you saw (hopefully a leopard!)
Lodge pool time — the pool overlooks the scrubland where animals sometimes wander past
Lunch at the lodge
Nap time
Visit nearby Kirinda Beach and ancient temple — the temple overlooks the Indian Ocean, peacocks roam the grounds
Final afternoon safari drive — one more chance for leopards, painted storks, jackals at dusk
Farewell dinner under the stars at Wild Coast — special bush dinner set up by the lodge
Listen to the jungle — leopards sometimes call at night near the lodge
Final safari morning walk (on foot within the lodge grounds, guided) — spot birds, tracks
Breakfast and check out of Wild Coast
Drive to Galle (3.5 hrs, coastal road, kids nap)
Arrive Amangalla — check into this exquisite Aman hotel inside Galle Fort's 17th-century walls
Lunch at Amangalla — colonial veranda dining, ceiling fans, impeccable Aman service
Nap time at the hotel — the rooms are serene, white-linen colonial elegance
Explore Galle Fort's interior — Dutch Reformed Church, maritime museum, boutique shops, gem stores
Watch the sunset from the Galle Fort lighthouse — local kids play cricket on the green below
Amangalla spa — candlelit treatment rooms, Ayurvedic oils, deeply relaxing
Farewell dinner at A Minute by Tuk Tuk — fusion restaurant inside the fort, creative Sri Lankan cuisine

Last walk on the Galle Fort ramparts — morning light on the Indian Ocean
Drive to Colombo airport (2.5 hrs)

tips: June/July is the dry season on the south coast — perfect timing for this itinerary
Sri Lanka is incredibly affordable even at luxury level — your dollar goes very far
Yala leopard safaris have the highest success rate of any destination — about 50% chance per game drive
Motion sickness meds recommended for the whale watching — the boats rock
Sri Lankan food is SPICY — ask for 'mild' for kids, and always have plain rice available
Tuk-tuk rides are a highlight for kids — short distances only, not on highways
Sri Lanka is safe for tourists but drive carefully — the roads can be chaotic
Elephant Transit Home is ethical (prepares elephants for wild release) — avoid any place offering elephant rides

name: Madeira, Portugal
tagline: Levada walks through cloud forests, toboggan rides down a mountain, and dolphins at dawn
weather: 65–78°F, subtropical warmth, occasional mountain clouds, sunny coast

hotel.name: Belmond Reid's Palace
highlights: Clifftop location overlooking the Atlantic since 1891
Three heated pools (one saltwater) with ocean views
Subtropical gardens with banana plants and bougainvillea
Afternoon tea tradition on the terrace — Winston Churchill's favorite hotel
Kids' club and babysitting available

Check in and settle — the hotel clifftop setting is immediately stunning

Dinner at William Restaurant — Portuguese cuisine, fresh Atlantic fish, passion fruit dessert

Nap time back at hotel
Hotel pool afternoon — three heated pools, ocean views, kids splash while grandparents sun
Dinner at The Dining Room — Belmond's fine dining with sunset views
Breakfast at hotel

Nap time at hotel
Walk through Funchal Old Town — painted doors of Rua de Santa Maria (each door is a unique art piece), cobblestone lanes, galleries

Sé Cathedral and nearby park — peaceful, grandparents enjoy the tilework
Dinner at Armazém do Sal — converted salt warehouse, creative Portuguese cuisine
Breakfast at hotel
Drive to Levada das 25 Fontes trailhead (1 hr) — scenic mountain road

Picnic lunch at the waterfall — packed by hotel
Walk back and nap time in the car — drive to Porto Moniz (30 min)

Dinner at hotel

Drive back down to coast — stop at a village bakery for bolo do caco (garlic bread)
Praia Formosa beach — Funchal's largest beach, dark sand, calm water areas for paddling, beach facilities
Beachside lunch — grilled fish, chips, salad
Nap time at hotel
Hotel pool and spa — grandparents get a massage, parents enjoy pool with kids
Afternoon tea on the terrace — second helping, it's that good
Dinner at Il Gallo d'Oro — Michelin-starred restaurant at the hotel
Breakfast at hotel

Santana Theme Park — gentle cultural park with water mills, craft workshops, traditional bread-making. Very toddler-friendly
Lunch in Santana — traditional espetada (beef on a laurel skewer) hung from a hook above the table
Nap time in the car — drive through the Laurissilva forest (UNESCO site)

Drive back to hotel
Final pool time at sunset
Dinner at Dona Amélia — Funchal's best traditional restaurant, in an old manor house
Leisurely breakfast
Walk to Câmara de Lobos fishing village (20 min along coastal promenade from hotel or 5 min taxi) — the village Churchill painted! Colorful boats, fishermen mending nets, dramatic cliff setting
Poncha tasting for adults at a local bar (traditional Madeiran drink: aguardente, honey, lemon) while kids have fresh orange juice
Cabo Girão Skywalk (15 min drive) — glass-floor viewpoint 580m above the ocean, second highest sea cliff in Europe. Hold toddler and don't look down! Thrilling for 5 yr old
Lunch at a cliff-top restaurant near Cabo Girão
Nap time at hotel
Blandy's Wine Lodge visit — Madeira wine tasting for adults (300 yr old wine cellars), grape juice for kids, beautiful gardens

Pack and check out

tips: The Monte toboggan ride is a must-do — book early morning to avoid queues
Levada walks are flat but narrow — use baby carriers, not strollers, and hold toddlers' hands near the water channels
Madeira's roads are winding — motion sickness meds for sensitive travelers

Poncha is deceptively strong — pace yourself, it tastes like fruit juice but packs a punch

tips: June/July is drier season in Sabah but expect afternoon thunderstorms — they pass quickly

name: Iceland — Golden Circle & Beyond
tagline: Geysers erupting, whales breaching, puffins posing, and the sun that never sets
weather: 45–60°F, midnight sun, unpredictable weather changes hourly

highlights: Private lagoon access — silica-rich geothermal water
In-room fireplaces with lava views
Moss Restaurant — fine dining with volcanic landscape views

Dinner at hotel
Breakfast at hotel

Lunch at a nearby farm restaurant

Dinner at hotel

Nap time in Reykjavik — find a quiet café or nap in the van

Drive back to Retreat

Retreat spa and lagoon morning — relax, read, enjoy thermal waters
Nap time
Lunch at hotel for those who stayed; packed lunch at glacier for hikers
Family reunites — Retreat lagoon together, silica mud masks (5 yr old loves the white face paint effect)
Lava tunnel tour nearby (Raufarhólshellir) — illuminated cave walk, easy path, dramatic
Dinner at Moss Restaurant — special tasting menu farewell
Breakfast at hotel

Nap time — drive around Reykjavik slowly, park somewhere scenic

Drive back to Retreat

Djúpalónssandur black pebble beach — lifting stones (adults try the old fisherman strength test!)

Nap time in the van
Kirkjufell mountain — Iceland's most photographed mountain, easy viewing area

Dinner at hotel — relaxed evening
Leisurely breakfast

Lunch at Lava Restaurant (public Blue Lagoon side) — different vibe, lava views
Nap time and packing

Late breakfast and check out — hotel is only 15 min from airport
Transfer to Keflavik Airport — duty-free shopping for Icelandic wool, chocolate

tips: Dress in layers — weather changes every 30 minutes. Wind-and-waterproof outer layer is essential for everyone
Bring waterproof pants for toddlers — they will want to splash in every puddle and stream

Midnight sun means blackout curtains are a must — bring clip-on travel blackout shades for kids
Motion sickness patches for the whale watching — even adults can get queasy
Iceland is extremely safe — tap water is pure glacial water, no dangerous animals exist

Pack light rain jackets for everyone; showers are frequent but usually short.

name: Lord Howe Island, Australia
tagline: A pristine island paradise limited to 400 visitors, with turtles on every beach
weather: 60–70°F (winter in Southern Hemisphere but mild), clear skies, gentle breezes

outbound.duration: 15h to Sydney + overnight + 2h to Lord Howe

return.arrive: July 5, 2026 10:00 AM local LAX (cross date line)

hotel.name: Capella Lodge
highlights: Only 9 suites — incredibly exclusive, max 18 guests on property
Gourmet meals included (breakfast, sunset drinks, multi-course dinner)
Overlooking the lagoon with twin volcanic peaks as backdrop
Complimentary bicycles, snorkel gear, kayaks, and paddleboards
No cars needed — the island is 11 km long, bikes go everywhere

schedule.activity: Overnight flight LAX → SYD (Qantas, 15 hrs) — try to sleep, kids usually pass out

days.title: Sydney Arrival & Onward to Lord Howe

schedule.activity: Arrive Sydney — transfer to domestic terminal
Flight SYD → LDH (QantasLink, 2 hrs) — small prop plane, stunning approach between twin peaks
Arrive Lord Howe Island — Capella Lodge pickup at the airstrip (5 min, the whole island is tiny)
Check in at Capella Lodge — welcome champagne, lodge orientation, room keys
Lunch at lodge — fresh seafood, garden salads
Nap time — massive jet lag, everyone sleeps
First beach walk — Ned's Beach (5 min bike ride). Hand-feed mullet, wrasse, and kingfish in knee-deep water. Kids throw fish food and watch dozens of fish swarm. Turtles often spotted
Sunset drinks at lodge deck — watching the sky turn colors over the lagoon
Dinner at Capella — multi-course, candlelit, lagoon views
Breakfast at lodge — eggs benedict, tropical fruits, fresh pastries
Glass-bottom boat tour (1.5 hrs) — see coral gardens, giant trevally, sea turtles through the glass. Perfect for grandparents and toddlers who can't snorkel. 5 yr old is spellbound
Lagoon Beach — wade in crystal-clear turquoise water. The lagoon is protected by reef, water is bathtub-calm. Perfect for toddlers. Parents snorkel along the reef edge
Lunch at lodge
Nap time
Bike ride to Old Settlement Beach — see woodhens (rare flightless birds endemic to the island) along the path
Turtle watching from the beach — green sea turtles feed in the shallows at this time of day
Sunset drinks
Dinner at lodge
Breakfast at lodge
Guided nature walk with island naturalist — Lord Howe woodhen (saved from extinction), providence petrels (so tame they land on your hand!), kentia palms. Easy flat trail, stroller-ok
Snorkeling at North Bay — boat ride to the reef, world-class snorkeling in 70+ species coral reef. Parents take turns, grandparents watch kids on the boat
Lunch at lodge
Nap time
Bike to the museum (Lord Howe Island Museum) — island history, whale bones, volcanic geology exhibits
Ned's Beach fish feeding again — kids can't get enough of the swarming fish
Sunset drinks and dinner at lodge
Early breakfast for hikers
Mount Gower guided hike — 8 hrs round trip, rated one of the best day hikes in the world. Cloud forest at the summit, endemic plants. Strenuous but unforgettable. One parent + one grandparent go
Leisurely breakfast for non-hikers
Beach morning at Lagoon Beach — paddleboarding, sandcastles, wading. Baby splashes in the shallows
Lunch at lodge
Nap time
Kayaking in the lagoon — calm water, 5 yr old rides in front of parent's kayak
Hikers return — triumphant and exhausted
Everyone reunites — stories at sunset drinks
Celebration dinner at lodge
Leisurely breakfast
Bike to Blinky Beach (east side) — surf beach, look for Galapagos sharks from the rocks (they patrol the shoreline, amazing to watch from above)
Malabar hill walk — short walk to a viewpoint with Balls Pyramid (world's tallest sea stack) in the distance
Lunch at Pinetrees Lodge restaurant (different lodge on the island) — change of scene
Nap time at Capella
Tide pool exploring at Middle Beach — sea urchins, starfish, tiny crabs
Final sunset fish feeding at Ned's Beach — the whole family, one last time
Farewell sunset drinks — watch the sun set behind the twin peaks
Final dinner at Capella — chef's special farewell menu
Final breakfast at Capella — savor every bite
Last beach walk — collect shells (allowed on Lord Howe), final turtle spotting
Pack and check out — the staff gathers to say goodbye (it's that kind of place)
Transfer to airstrip — light lunch at the island café
Flight LDH → SYD (2 hrs)
Arrive Sydney — check into Shangri-La Hotel near Circular Quay
Short walk to see Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge — quick photo stop
Dinner at Quay Restaurant or casual option in The Rocks
Early bedtime — fly home tomorrow
Breakfast at hotel — views of the harbor
Quick visit to Taronga Zoo (ferry across the harbor) — Australian animals: koalas, kangaroos, platypus. 1.5 hrs max
Transfer to Sydney Airport (30 min)
Flight SYD → LAX (13 hrs, arrive same day due to date line)

tips: Lord Howe limits visitors to 400 at any time — book Capella Lodge many months in advance
The QantasLink plane to Lord Howe is small (34 seats) with strict 14 kg luggage limit per person — pack light
It's winter in the Southern Hemisphere (June-July) but Lord Howe is subtropical — 60-70°F and pleasant
No cell service on much of the island — embrace the digital detox. Lodge has Wi-Fi
Bikes are the main transport — child seats and trailers available for rent at the lodge
The lagoon is the world's southernmost coral reef — snorkel gear is free at the lodge

costBreakdown.food: $2,000 (most included at Capella)

name: Slovenia — Lake Bled
tagline: A fairy-tale alpine lake with a church on an island and caves hiding castles
weather: 60–78°F, warm sunny days with occasional afternoon showers

hotel.name: Grand Hotel Toplice
highlights: Private thermal pool fed by natural springs
Direct lake access with private beach
On-site spa with alpine treatments
Family-friendly restaurant with kids' menu
Walking distance to Bled Castle and boat dock

Breakfast at hotel

Coffee and cake at a riverside café
Drive back to Bled

Cable car back down, drive to Bled — nap time in the van

tips: Bring baby carriers — strollers work on flat paths but not on gorge boardwalks or castle steps

name: Finnish Lakeland
tagline: Saunas on the lake, wild blueberries, and the sun that refuses to set
weather: 55–75°F, midnight sun (barely sets), occasional rain showers

outbound.duration: 11h direct to Helsinki + 4h drive or 1h domestic flight

Private lake beach with dock and rowboats
Wine cellar carved into bedrock

Nap time

tips: Pack insect repellent — Finnish lakes have mosquitoes in summer, especially at dawn and dusk
The midnight sun is magical but disorienting — bring sleep masks and blackout curtains for kids
Finnish tap water is some of the purest in the world — drink freely from any tap

Finland consistently ranks as the world's safest country — relax and let kids explore

name: Patagonia (Argentina/Chile)

hotel.name: Explora Patagonia (Torres del Paine, 4 nights) + Eolo Lodge (El Calafate, 3 nights)

days.title: Arrive Punta Arenas — Transfer to Torres del Paine

Stargazing — southern hemisphere skies, Magellanic Clouds visible
Breakfast at Explora

Grey Lake viewpoint — see the Grey Glacier, icebergs floating in milky-blue water
Picnic lunch prepared by Explora — hot soup in thermoses, sandwiches, chocolate, all set up at a viewpoint
Nap time in the car — driving back, toddlers sleep, scenery is mesmerizing for adults

Explora van to Mirador Cuernos — short walk to the viewpoint of the iconic Cuernos del Paine
Free time at Explora — reading room, games, hot chocolate by the fire

Explore the estancia grounds — spot Patagonian hares, flamingos on the lagoon, upland geese
Wine tasting at Eolo — Argentine Malbec, Torrontés, paired with local cheeses
Dinner at Eolo — ranch-style dining, beef tenderloin, chimichurri, homemade ice cream
Southern sky stargazing from the heated patio

tips: June/July is WINTER in Patagonia — pack serious cold-weather gear: down jackets, thermal layers, waterproof boots for everyone

tips: July is excellent for Mara — the Great Migration begins arriving; dry weather, cooler temps, incredible wildlife density.
Children under 5 may not be allowed on game drives at some camps — confirm age policies. Angama Mara and Governors' Camp are family-friendly.
Hot air balloon rides have a minimum age (usually 7) — adults-only activity.
Malaria prophylaxis is essential for all family members. Consult your pediatrician for appropriate medication for toddlers.
Pack warm layers for early morning game drives — it can be 55°F at dawn.
The 1-year-old and 2.5-year-old will need entertainment during long game drives — bring small quiet toys.
Bush planes have strict luggage limits (15kg soft bags) — pack light.
Yellow fever vaccination may be required — check current Kenya entry requirements.
Travel insurance with medical evacuation coverage is essential — nearest hospital is Nairobi.

tips: June–July is Cape Town's winter — expect cool, rainy weather (46–60°F). Pack warm layers, rain jackets, and waterproof shoes for everyone.
Table Mountain cable car closes in high wind — have a backup plan. Morning typically has calmer wind.
Shark cage diving has a minimum age (usually 10+) and is weather-dependent. Only for adventurous adults.
The Aquila safari is a 2-hr drive each way — consider this for toddler tolerance. Some reserves offer fly-in options.
South Africa has world-class baby infrastructure — diapers, formula, and supplies are readily available in Cape Town.
Safety: stick to tourist areas, use private transfers (not ride-shares), and don't flash valuables. The V&A Waterfront area is very safe.
No malaria in Cape Town — but needed if you add a Kruger extension.
The 1-year-old rides free on most activities; the 2.5-year-old may need a paid seat on the cable car.
Boulders Beach is stroller-accessible via the boardwalk — a must-do with kids of any age.

outbound.class: Business / Economy (no business on FAE leg)

tips: Weather changes rapidly in the Azores — bring layers and rain gear even on sunny mornings.
Two rental cars (or a large van) are needed for 4 adults + 3 car seats + luggage.
The thermal pool at Terra Nostra stains light-colored swimsuits orange (iron-rich water) — bring dark swimwear.
Whale watching has high success rates in June–July but seas can be rough — bring motion sickness medication.
Toddlers should not go on whale watching boats — too long, too bumpy. Split the group.
Fumaroles are genuinely dangerous — boiling water and toxic gases. Hold toddlers' hands at ALL times near caldeiras.
The Azores are very affordable compared to mainland Portugal — great value for families.
Driving is the best way to explore — roads are good but winding. Drive slowly.
Baby supplies are available in Ponta Delgada pharmacies and supermarkets.

tips: Pack layers and rain gear for everyone — Haida Gwaii is cool and wet even in summer. Temperatures rarely exceed 65°F.

tips: Passports required for ALL travelers including infants — ensure baby's passport is current.
Chileno Bay is one of the only swimmable beaches in Los Cabos — most Cabo beaches have dangerous rip currents.
June/July is hot (90°F+) — schedule outdoor activities for early morning. Afternoon shade/AC is essential for littles.
The villa option is ideal for families with small children: private pool, kitchen for snacks/bottles, and space to spread out.
Whale shark season typically ends in June — confirm availability before booking. If unavailable, the sea lion colony is equally spectacular.
Cabo Pulmo is remote — bring all supplies (diapers, snacks, water, sunscreen). No stores nearby.
Flora Farms and Acre are destination restaurants — book reservations 2–3 weeks in advance.
Tap water is not safe to drink in Mexico — use bottled water for everything including brushing teeth and mixing formula.
In-villa private chef service is a game-changer with small kids — no car rides to restaurants, kids can go to bed on schedule.
The short 2.5-hour flight from LAX is a huge advantage with three small children compared to 5+ hour flights.

tips: Ko Olina's lagoons are the single best beach feature for families with toddlers on any Hawaiian island — calm, enclosed, no waves.
Pearl Harbor tickets sell out weeks in advance — book at recreation.gov as soon as your dates are confirmed.
Hanauma Bay now requires advance reservations — book online. Closed Mondays and Tuesdays.
The Polynesian Cultural Center is a full afternoon + evening commitment (~6 hrs). Worth it, but plan for it to be the main event that day.
Ko Olina is on the leeward (west) side — drier and sunnier than Honolulu/Waikiki. Less crowded too.
Helena's Hawaiian Food closes at 8 PM and is cash only. Go early with kids.
For the shark cage diving, one parent pair goes while the other stays with kids. Or grandparents can join the boat without entering the cage.
Marukame Udon line looks intimidating but moves quickly — usually 15-20 min wait. Worth it.
Four Seasons Ko Olina is 30-40 min from Honolulu/Waikiki attractions — factor in drive time.
Bring a lightweight stroller for excursions and a baby carrier for beach/uneven terrain.
The resort's complimentary kids' club is excellent for giving the 5yo social time with other children while parents manage the younger ones.
